Title: Board Game Cafe - Birmingham
Summary:
An evening of board games, food, drink and probably polite company.  Join Outdoorlads for an evening at the popular board game cafe in Birmingham.
There's a small cover charge of £2.50 per person, per hour. This is paid when the leader books the table, so expect a message the day before to confirm your attendnace.  We will likely play in small groups of 5.
If the event is full, join the waiting list and I'll book a additional tables if there is enough interest.
Social events are great way to forge new friendships and talk about your experiences on OutdoorLads events, and to find out what is going on in the coming months.
Want to keep in touch with what's going on in the Midlands / ODL Central Region?   Sign up for
Central Region Events Notifications (Telegram Channel)
and the
Central Region Telegram Chat Group
, and bookmark our upcoming events list via the short link:
https://outdoorlads.events/central
.
Photos by Outdoorwill and used with his permission.
Difficulty & Distance: Social

Title: High Peak ‘in-steam’ Heritage Day Walk
Summary:
We will meet at Cromford Wharf Carpark, a short walk from Cromford Railway Station at 10.30am. I’ll be arriving at Cromford Mill, over the road from the carpark an hour beforehand so if you get in earlier they’ll be a chance to join me for a short tour.
A challenging walk taking in beautiful scenery, an abundance of wildlife, cracking views, and world renowned heritage, the Derwent Valley Mills having been awarded World Heritage Status in 2001. Cromford Canal is a haven for wildlife and a Site of Special Scientific Interest with little grebes and water voles, dragonflies, damselflies and hoverflies.
From Cromford Mill where there are a number of interesting buildings, we will follow the towpath from Cromford Wharf for one and a quarter miles to Leawood Pumping House.  The Pumping House was built in 1849 to pump water from the River Derwent into the canal, it operated for almost 100 years.
The walk has been planned to coincide with Heritage Open Days 2026 and a steaming day of two beam engines - Leawood Pumping Station and Middleton Top Engine House.
Experience the power of steam, the heat of the boilers, the smell of grease and coal and the power of the wave as tons of water are pumped...
Following this we will cross the canal walking between the old railway buildings of the High Peak Junction to ascend the steep High Peak Trail. At the foot of the incline is a catch pit, where runaway wagons once came to rest. On reaching the summit and Engine House we will be rewarded with views over the Derwent Valley. Remaining on the High Peak Trail we will aim to lunch at Black Rocks Visitor Centre (drinks and home-made cakes available here). Then onto Middleton Top where we will leave the Trail and walk though the historic limestone village of Middleton-by-Wirksworth before descending to meet the Via Gellia road and then onto Water Lane and Scarthin with views over Cromford Dam.
Those of you who would like to stay on might enjoy Arkwright’s Mill, Cromford village and/or a village pub to enjoy a well-earned pint or two before heading home.
All photos taken by Christopher Chambers, used with permission
Difficulty & Distance: 9.75 Miles / 16km

Title: New Forest - Paddle to Pub
Summary:
Join us for a relaxing paddle along one of the South Coast’s most beautiful waterways.
We’ll launch onto the tranquil Beaulieu River and take our time exploring this picturesque stretch of the New Forest, gliding past salt marshes, ancient woodland and the occasional curious wildlife. Whether you’re a seasoned paddler or simply looking for a laid-back way to enjoy the outdoors, this is the perfect opportunity to get out on the water with fellow OutdoorLads members.
Our journey will take us to the historic riverside hamlet of
Buckler’s Hard
, where we’ll pull ashore at the famous
Master Builder’s Pub
. Here you’ll have the chance to relax with a drink, enjoy some lunch, and soak up the atmosphere of this unique maritime setting before making our return.
A gentle paddle, great company, beautiful scenery and a pub by the river – what more could you want from a sunny weekend?
Difficulty & Distance: Kayaking to the pub!

Title: Newcastle Autumn Social
Summary:
Join Ste in the great northern city of Newcastle Upon Tyne for a few drinks in good company following on from the
North East and Yorkshire leaders' event
.
We will be visiting venues in the cool Quayside area before venturing to the gay scene music bars for the full Toon experience and an opportunity for night owls to party into the small hours.
There will be plenty of time to chat, to give newer members an introduction to our great charity and an opportunity to meet some of our amazing event leaders.
The event should give you a taste of the great atmosphere and the amazing popular venues that keep Newcastle up there as a top weekend party destination.
Bring your humour and good spirits and you all will be very welcome.
Image Credits: Photos provided by event leaders, Simon and Steven. OutdoorLads has permission to use them.
Difficulty & Distance: Short walks between venues

Title: Yorkshire and North East Leaders' Gathering 2026 - Newcastle
Summary:
This is an opportunity for leaders who regularly lead events in our region to get to know each other, meet your coordinator and team of organisers, exchange ideas, ask questions and help to plan our programme of events for 2027.  If you are not a leader but have an application in progress you are also welcome to attend.
Buffet lunch and drinks will be available at the venue. As a thank you to our leaders, OutdoorLads will make a contribution towards the cost of your lunch.
Please join us for our
Newcastle Social
in the evening.
Image credits: all images provided by the event leader: OutdoorLads has permission to use them.

Title: BIG20: New Forest Celebration Weekend!
Summary:
It's OutdoorLads' 20th anniversary! Come join us for a Summer weekend in the New Forest to celebrate this huge milestone... the BIG 20 events this year are a showcase of some of our very best events from the last 20 years, where we can enjoy a weekend celebrating all that OutdoorLads has to offer with some great activities and a few little surprises to mark the occasion, too!
We have a full weekend of events, the event pages will be bookable from 5pm on the 12th June!

(walk)

(paid for event)

(paid for event)
Sat 18/07   1:15pm -

New Forest Walk

Choose Your Accommodation
Bunkhouse - we have used this site a number of times and you can expect dormitory style accommodation with some spare beds available allowing us to spread-out throughout the building, there is a decent size lounge and dining area.  Showers are in a separate block 10 metres from the Bunkhouse
Camping - we have been able to secure options for camping alongside the hostel - see the
camping page
for full details.
For campers, the event price still includes all meals (2 x breakfasts, and evening meal Saturday night)
Itinerary
The following is all subject to change but should give you an idea of what to expect if this is your first ODL hostel event.
Friday
You'll be able to arrive at the site from around 4.30pm on the Friday, and find a bed in the dorms. Once settled in, there is plenty of room to relax in the lounge, with hot drinks available throughout the weekend. If your preference is for an alcoholic tipple you'll need to bring these yourself.
Friday evening will be the traditional OutdoorLads buffet. Bring along something to feed one or two people, and home-cooked is always preferred.
Once we've had a briefing over the plans for the weekend, we'll be looking for some volunteers to tidy up the kitchen, whilst the others can head back to relax in the lounge (or outside if its warm!), chat to others or perhaps enjoy some board games (don't forget to bring your favourites!)
Saturday
After breakfast on Saturday morning you'll head out on your choice of activity (listed above), returning to our accommodation early afternoon. This means you will likely need a packed lunch that you will need to bring yourself.
For those of you that like your Parkrun on a Saturday morning, Brockenhurst has you covered and we'll time our daytime activities to give you a chance to do both.
OutdoorLads hostel events always rely on everyone mucking in and helping in the kitchen over the weekend, so whilst a few volunteers are busy in the kitchen, we'll hopefully have some anniversary themed activities in the lounge.
We will provide dinner for the Saturday night, so please make sure your dietary requirements are up to date. We'll also raise a glass to OutdoorLads tonight with dinner! We will need some volunteers to clear up in the kitchen and dining room, but then it's a chance to natter to fellow OutdoorLads, listen to some music and likely that there will be board games available to partake in. Being mid-Summer, lets hope it's nice and we can enjoy being outside again, too!
Sunday
Start the day with breakfast and as much tea/coffee as needed to recover from the night before, and then we'll spend an hour or so tidying up the venue and leaving it as clean as we found it, we will need to be out by midday.
Our plan is to offer a short walk on the Sunday (listed above) for those that are not in a hurry to leave or you can go and explore this beautiful part of the south coast.
Good to Know....
Dogs
Sorry, this event is not suitable for dogs. Registered animals are welcome, but please let us know at the time of booking if you will be bringing an assistance dog.
Car Sharing
The is parking available but sharing is good, so if driving to the event please consider offering spare seats to other members heading the same way.
OutdoorLads Foundation
Fancy coming along but having a difficult time of it (for whatever reason), and/or finding it hard to justify the expense? Apply for a place on this event through the OutdoorLads Foundation! Nobody apart from staff members will know and we don't need lots of financial info from you either. To apply through the Foundation, you'll need to complete a very short form, which can be found along with more information
here
.
Want to Pay By Instalments?
You can arrange to pay in instalments if that helps. Please contact the office on
support@outdoorlads.com
who will help you to secure a place and set up paying in instalments. The event must be fully paid up a week before the event takes place.

Title: Edinburgh Park Sculpture Walk
Summary:
Who would have thought a walk round a business park would be interesting?
Well the owners of Edinburgh Park have spent a few quid on some sculptures and landscaping to make for a more pleasant environment for the workers in the west of Edinburgh. The walk is an evening circular around the lochans that play host to Mallards and Moorhens, and we could also stop into Patina Bakery for a coffee on our way back to the station.
The main event however are the sculptures - there are two 'sets' to view - the large arty pieces that can be largely found round the 1 Park Square building, and twelve heads of Scottish poets that are on the walk around the lochans.

Title: Parkend, Lydney & Yorkley
Summary:
Starting at Parkend in the Forest of Dean we'll be enjoying some woodland and open walks heading towards Lydney where if the weather is good we'll enjoy a packed lunch enjoying the views over the Severn or perhaps we may even enjoy a pint in one of the local establishments.  After lunch we're heading down and then back up the other side of quite a steep valley to enjoy more views out over the surrounding countryside before heading back through the woodland to Parkend where we cross over the Dean Forest Railway station.   To celebrate an enjoyable day in the Forest we'll head to one of the local pubs for a drink.
Dogs will be welcome on the walk but will need to be kept under control.
Want to Get Out More?

.

Title: Doddington Hall Leisure Walk
Summary:
Join Chris for a 5.5-mile (9 km) circular walk beginning and ending at Doddington Hall. We set off along the former track bed of the Lancashire, Derbyshire & East Coast Railway, now a quiet green corridor leading us gently away from the Hall and through peaceful countryside. The route soon crosses the county boundary, leaving Lincolnshire briefly for Nottinghamshire, before reaching the village of Harby.
Harby is known for its association with Queen Eleanor of Castile, who died here in 1290. We pause at The Bottle & Glass, a friendly village pub, before continuing.
From Harby, the path leads across open farmland, offering wide views across the surrounding landscape. The route then returns to Doddington Hall, where the striking Elizabethan house, Tudor Gatehouse and landscaped gardens form a fitting highlight at the end of the walk.
After the walk, there is the opportunity to attend
Lincoln Pride
, or alternatively, you can explore the house and gardens at Doddington Hall by
purchasing a ticket
.
For those interested in a Parkrun before the event, the nearest one is at
Doddington Hall
.
What is Lincoln Pride?
Lincoln Pride
is an annual celebration of the LGBTQ+ community held in the city of Lincoln. It brings together people of all backgrounds to promote equality, diversity and inclusion through a vibrant day of music, performances and community activities. Local organisations, charities and independents come together to offer support, information and resources, while the event also provides a welcoming space for people to express themselves and be proud of who they are. Lincoln Pride is not just a festival, it is a positive and visible reminder that everyone deserves respect, acceptance and the freedom to live authentically.
Your First OutdoorLads Event?
We know that new members in particular like to know in advance more about what an OutdoorLads event will be like.
If this is your first OutdoorLads event, you should check out the information designed specifically for you on the
My First Event
page on the OutdoorLads website.
What if there is a Waiting List?
If this event is fully booked and you want to attend, just add yourself to the waitlist, because places inevitably become available closer to the date.

Title: Evening climbing in the Peaks
Summary:
Come and join OutdoorLads an evening  rock climbing at Windgather.
Windgather Crag
is just a short walk from the roadside car park, around 5 km from Whaley Bridge, and is easily accessible from Sheffield, Manchester, Stoke-on-Trent, Macclesfield and Crewe. Chinley is also on the Manchester–Sheffield train line.
This event is suitable for all abilities and Beginners are especially Welcome!!
Never climbed outdoors before, or need a refresher? You're in the right place! Our leaders will show you the ropes, and you'll be climbing in no time. We'll set up several top ropes with bottom belays for everyone to use throughout the day, similar to what you'd find at an indoor climbing wall.
Climbing is a full-body workout. It's not just about strength; it also requires balance, problem-solving and planning. Outdoor climbing is a great way to stay fit, make new friends and enjoy the fresh air. This event will help you develop your climbing skills in a supportive environment.
For those who have climbed before (typically leading and lead-belaying indoors to around 6a) and want to develop their outdoor climbing, we'll have more experienced climbers for you to partner with. You'll be able to second routes and learn how to remove protection as you climb.
If you're an experienced climber with your own harness, helmet, rack and rope, we'll pair you with other competent climbers so you can choose and climb your own routes.
If you're not sure whether this event is right for you, just message the
event leader
, who'll be happy to answer any questions and help you decide.
As with any outdoor activity, we'll need to remain flexible depending on the weather, the availability of routes and the experience of everyone attending. The event may be adapted or, in exceptional circumstances, cancelled if weather conditions require, so please keep an eye on your messages in the days leading up to the event for any updates from the event leader.
Difficulty & Distance: Outdoor climbing for all abilities, but a moderate level of fitness is required.

Title: Outdoor Climbing - Stanage Edge (Beginners Welcome)
Summary:
Come and join OutdoorLads rock climbing at Stanage Edge
One of the most iconic and well loved crags in the Peak District. With an abundance of quality routes across a wide range of grades, there is something here for everyone, whether you're new to outdoor climbing or a seasoned gritstone enthusiast.
This event is suitable for all abilities - Beginners especially Welcome!!
Never climbed outdoors before or need a refresher? You're in the right place, our leaders will show you the ropes and you'll be climbing the crags in no time. There are also many lead climbs available too if you're an experienced climber!
The crag offers a variety of climbs, and the team will ensure that you both enjoy your climbing experience but are also challenged.
For beginners, we’ll have a couple of climbing leaders available, along with the kit and instruction you’ll need to get you climbing your first routes, safely, on real rock. Instruction with be given for pre-rigged climbs - just like you'd find indoors at your local climbing wall.
For those who have climbed before (typically lead-belaying and climbing on-lead indoors to around 6A) and who want to develop their climbing, there may (depending on availability) be the opportunity to partner up with an experienced climber and second a route.
If you're an experienced lead climber with your own harness, helmet, rack and rope, then (if other experienced climbers are available) we'll try to pair you up with other competent climbers to make your own choice of routes.
There may also be the opportunity to abseil/rappel down a selected route for those who wish to try this.
If you're not sure if this event is for you - just message the event leader who'll be happy to help and answer any questions.
Those coming along to this climbing event who are are on the Hope Valley Camp should meet us at the campsite at 8:30am, ready to  leave shortly after that with the aim of arriving at Hollin Bank car park by 9am. Anyone coming along to this climbing event who isn't camping with us should meet us at Hollin Bank car park at 9am.
Naturally, we'll need to be flexible in how we run this event, depending on conditions on the day, availability of routes on the crag, and the abilities of everyone else attending, and this event maybe subject to change / cancellation depending on weather conditions, so please check your messages in the days before the event for any message from the event leader if that may be the case.
Image credits - all images provided by OutdoorLads members. OutdoorLads has permission to use them.
Difficulty & Distance: All climbing activity is physical, this event is suitable for Beginner & Intermediate climbers

We'll start the day with a hearty breakfast at a great café in Chinley, just a few miles from the crag. It's a great way to support a local business while we plan our routes for the day.
Of course, if you're not a breakfast person, you're more than welcome to meet us at the crag car park at around 12:00.

Title: Eight Days a Week(end) of Fun: A Liverpool City Break
Summary:
*That sold quickly please join the waitlist as we are hoping to add a 3rd room which will increase our spaces by 6
*
Come and join us for a weekend break in Liverpool, and enjoy fun times exploring the city!
Liverpool is a vibrant maritime city in North West England, renowned as the birthplace of The Beatles, a powerhouse of football culture, and a hub of rich industrial history. Situated on the Mersey Estuary, it features UNESCO-listed docks, diverse cultural attractions, stunning architecture, and a lively, welcoming atmosphere. Liverpool is the most filmed city outside of London, recent productions filmed here include Batman, Captain America, Sherlock Holmes and Peaky Blinders.
City breaks are less formal and structured than our usual hostel breaks, you’ll have free time or time to do the suggested activities below, but they are all suggestions. City breaks do not have the usual Friday night buffet, (and indeed no food or drink is included in the price) however places to eat as a group will be suggested, or you may wish do your own thing or break into smaller groups.
Itinerary:
Day 1:
Arrive late afternoon on Friday, meet & greet reception before heading off out for dinner and drinks. (Exact restaurant location to be confirmed nearer event date)
Day 2:
Saturday morning, Optional Breakfast at the hostel before we head out for a walking tour exploring the city's maritime heritage. We may stop for lunch at the Royal Albert Docks. If time we might take a trip on the
ferry across the Mersey!
In the afternoon there will be free time to explore the city with an optional visit to the
Beatles Museum
In the evening we will reconvene at the hostel before joining a local OutdoorLads social! Exploring the city's vibrant night life.
Day 3:
Sunday morning, Optional Breakfast at the hostel then free time before checkout - option of a visit to
Liverpool Cathedral
or the
Walker Art Gallery,
Or some adult crazy golf at
Golf Fang Liverpool
Accommodation
Hostel accommodation - 2 x  6 bed ensuite rooms which will only be shared with other OutdoorLads members attending the city break.
Please note that unlike most of our other hostel events, food is not provided and we will not have sole use of the hostel.
Dogs:
Unfortunately this event is not suitable for dogs. Registered assistance dogs welcome, but please let us know when you book.
Photos:
GeekyJames & Cassidyi, used by OutdoorLads with permission.
Difficulty & Distance: Weekend City Break & Social

Title: The Weekend Gathering
Summary:
Scottish Leaders  - this weekend is just for you!
In a change to our usual routine of biannual meetings, we've blown the budget by booking a comfortable hostel for a couple of nights, which is heavily subsidised by OutdoorLads.
It's a chance to celebrate the organisation, our positive impact on our community and how volunteers make it happen. A chance to connect and network with other leaders, share ideas and tips, inspiration, motivation and plans. It's also a thank you from OutdoorLads to you for all you put into the organisation, without which - there would be no OutdoorLads! But don't worry - this IS NOT a whole weekend of Alan droning on about how last month had three more attendees than last year.... we'll also be getting outside, partaking in activities and getting to know each other!
We are planning to make full use of the weekend - Friday evening will be an opportunity to socialise with most of the rest of the Scottish leader team; Saturday will be a full mixed day discussing challenges and opportunities while also getting out for a walk and talk; and we may have a short session on Sunday morning before heading home.
This weekend is aimed at leaders in Scotland, should space become available leaders from elsewhere who were unable to attend the similar events we ran in February and March 2026 are also welcome to attend, especially those based in the far north of England.
To note:
This event is heavily subsidised by OutdoorLads, and food is provided over the weekend. Travel expenses are not provided for this event. If that cost is a struggle for you, we don't want that to be a reason for you not to join us - please apply to the
OutdoorLads Foundation
for help with that cost.
Although the standard refund policy applies to this event, if you need to cancel, please do so as early as possible, so we can ensure the event is as full as possible with leaders who can benefit from the weekend.
If you are not yet a leader you will need to have applied and be well on with your leader application to join this event. If you're not sure if you should book or not, drop the office a line on
support@outdoorlads.com
and we'll chat it through with you. We will remove any bookings from members who are not leaders and haven't got an active leader application in.
Photos:
by AMD and used with permission.
Dogs:
This event is not suitable for dogs.
Difficulty & Distance: Varied programme on offer, depends on what you do - but expect to be outside taking part in activities!

Title: London Indoor Climbing Evening - All abilities- Beginners welcome
Summary:
Come join OutdoorLads for indoor climbing in North London!
At the biggest climbing centre in North London, there are 450 routes over 95 lines set across 1000 square metres of wall offering top-roping, lead climbing and bouldering on a wide range of slabs, cracks, overhangs, off-widths and chimneys - there really is something to suit every level of climbing ability!
I've never climbed before, can I come along? YES- but contact the Event Leader (see below)!
Supervised sessions
If you're unsure whether you need to be signed in as a supervised climber or not, if you answer
NO
to any of the following three questions, then you will need to arrange to be supervised. If you can answer YES to all these questions, you can sign up to this event as an unsupervised climber:
1. Can you fit a climbing harness correctly?
2. Can you tie yourself to the end of a rope using the correct knots?
3. Can you belay another climber correctly and confidently?
To come along, you will need to register via this event page, and to climb you will need to pay an entry fee directly to the centre when you arrive.
See below.
Also to make climbing more accessible, the Castle has kindly provided us with some group benefits:
If you are climbing with the group, tell the reception when paying for entry and you will get the concession rate. That's £12.50 instead of £18 for full price entry!
For those in need of financial support, we have access to some free entries. Speak to the Event Leader about how this works. We need to provide to the Castle some basic details (name, DoB, email) 48 hours before the session.

Title: The Rolling Dales of the Yorkshire Wolds
Summary:
An exploration of the Yorkshire Wolds in a much underrated area of Yorkshire approximately 20 miles east of the city of York, starting in the pretty village of Millington and visiting Givendale, Grimthorpe Wood, Mile Fields, Pocklington Woods and crossing the greens of the local golf course. Watch out for the balls!
It's a chance for a sociable walk where paths are mostly wide enough for two or more. The route incorporates parts of the Yorkshire Wolds Way, Minster Way and Chalkland Way. This is a walk I'm repeating from previous years, and is my favourite route I can walk directly from home.
The route has some hills, but only a total height gain of 500m. However, can be a little challenging on a hot day.
From the top of the wolds at Millington you can get wide views across the vale of York, (on a clear day you can see as far as the Kilburn White Horse at Sutton Bank, almost 40 miles away). The walk also takes in some of the many pretty dales of the Yorkshire Wolds.
There is a chance for a tea stop about three quarters of the way round or at the end of the walk (there is also a pub in millington).
If you arrive early or forget your lunch, there is a Co-op and at least one cafe that opens early in Pocklington. There is free parking in available on Sundays. If you want to come via bus (East Yorkshire Motor Services EYMS app shows times and routes) please message me or others for a lift from Pocklington, as there is no direct bus to Millington village.
If the event is fully booked:
please join the waiting list as people frequently drop out during the week before an event and you are likely to get a place on the walk.
Difficulty & Distance: A twelve miles with a few moderate climbs.

Title: Walk the wailing Wain Stones
Summary:
Total distance: about 18 km (approx. 11.2 miles), with 710 m (2325 feet) of ascent. Approximately 6 hours, including lunch. This walk includes some steep climbs and descents, as well as relatively flat sections (moorland). There is little shade for most of the walk, and given the elevation, it is usually windy.
Starting from the Clay Bank, we ascend towards the coast-to-coast path. This initial section takes us up above Hasty Bank (398 m) with magnificent views North, towards Middlesbrough and beyond. We then walk along a relatively flat stone path to the Wainstones.
The name "Wainstones" (a prominent sandstone outcrop on Hasty Bank in the North York Moors) is believed to originate from the ancient Anglo-Saxon word wanian, meaning to lament or grieve. Historically, the stones were called the "Stones of Lamentation", and legend suggests they may have been a burial site for a slain Danish chieftain. Another theory is that the jumbled, rock-strewn gap was simply viewed as a place to mourn or perform rituals. Alternatively, the name is sometimes linked to the archaic word "wain" (a poetic term for a wagon or cart), as the rocks' shape can vaguely resemble an old carriage from certain angles.
After a descent, and a short valley section, we are soon climbing again, this time towards Cold Moor (401 m). Despite the name, it was warm when I visited ;)
Continuing on, we climb above Carlton Bank to reach Cringle Moor before heading on to the Lord Stones.
The "
Lord Stones
" refer to a prominent collection of ancient boundary markers and prehistoric stones. The name originates from the Three Lords' Stone, which historically marked the point where the estates of three local lords met.
Bronze Age Barrow
: The primary stone sits atop the perimeter of an ancient Bronze Age burial mound. This area formed a small cemetery and ceremonial site thousands of years before the local estates were formed
We then reach
Lordstones cafe,
where refreshments can be bought. There are benches and a large flat area nearby, for those who bring a packed lunch.
After another more gradual ascent, we cross an exposed section of moorland. Our gradual descent takes us through a short section under trees. We then resume a gradual ascent towards Urra Moor. This section passes through an area where many partridges breed, so any dogs need to be kept under close control. We return to the path we started along earlier in the day, for a final 1.5 miles or so back to the car park.
For those who may like a drink after, the best local option is
The Dudley Arms
at Ingelby Greenhow, less than 3 miles away.
Dogs: Well-behaved, under control dogs are welcome at this event.
Dogs should be kept on a lead whenever signposted, whenever we are in the vicinity of livestock, and at the event leader's request. Many stretches of this walk include sheep roaming. I would also note that there are a few up-and-over wall stiles towards the end of the walk, so you get a bit of an upper-body workout lifting your dogs over them. You'll need to make sure you're carrying sufficient extra water for your four-legged friend. We do not encounter much natural water on this walk. If you're unsure, contact the leader for guidance.
If the event is fully booked:
Please join the waiting list as people frequently drop out during the week before an event, and you are likely to get a place on the walk.
If this is your first OutdoorLads walk event,
we’ll make sure you feel welcome. Please
check out the My First Event page
, and don’t hesitate to drop me a message if you have questions or want to know more about this walk.
Image/photo credits:
All leader's own images - with permission for OutdoorLads to use.
Difficulty & Distance: 11+ miles, several steep ascents and descents.

Title: Sheffield Bouldering @ The Climbing Hanger
Summary:
Swap trainers for climbing shoes and join OutdoorLads for an evening of INDOOR BOULDERING at The Climbing Hanger in Sheffield.
Cards/contactless ONLY  - this venue does not accept cash
If you haven't been to The Hangar before, please click
here
to pre-register. Click on the link, scroll down, select Sheffield and follow the on-screen instructions. Completing the waiver prior to arrival will get you climbing faster.
What is Bouldering?
It's a form of climbing where instead of long high routes, the 'problems' tend to be shorter and focus on technique, balance, body positioning and sometimes power. Because of this different emphasis, bouldering doesn't have the hassle of climbing with ropes, harnesses and belaying, which makes it an excellent activity for newcomers to the sport. No wonder it's often regarded as the 'purest' form of climbing.
If you're a seasoned climber you won't need convincing about the benefits of bouldering, but for those who've never climbed more than a staircase, the benefits are wide ranging: from the physical - improved strength, tone, balance, power and coordination; mental - problem solving, focus, concentration and body awareness; to the emotional - relaxation, a sense of achievement and a feeling that mind and body are working together perfectly ('feeling psyched'). On top of all this, bouldering tends to be a very social sport where you can hang out with mates and each have a go as you feel regardless of what level you climb at.
I'm new to Bouldering - can I come along?
If you have never tried bouldering before it is recommended that you have a guided session from the event leader, who will go through everything you will need to know to boulder safely before joining in with the rest of the group.
I've been Bouldering before?
As above, everyone attending the event  must abide by the centre's T&Cs etc. As an experienced boulderer you responsible for your own safety, actions and involvement at all times. As such, you should use your own judgement in taking up advice given either by OutdoorLads Volunteers or other experienced climbers.
Photos : Copyright granted to OutdoorLads by the event leader / venue.
Please note that every person participating should be aware of (and accept) that those members perceived as ‘in charge’ or leading the event are not necessarily qualified, do not need to be qualified, and are not regarded by OutdoorLads, or themselves as ‘technical experts’ but are amateur climbers with some experience and who are happy to impart their knowledge. Any advice given should be considered with this in mind by the recipient.
All those attending the event must also be aware of the centre's own terms and conditions of participation and abide by them, along with any instructions given by the centre’s staff. Everyone must also take responsibility for their own safety while participating in Indoor climbing events.
Difficulty & Distance: Suitable for all abilities. If you're new to bouldering, just let the event leader know and we'll get you started!

On top of all this, bouldering tends to be a very social sport where you can hang out with mates and each have a go as you feel regardless of what level you climb at.
I'm new to Bouldering - can I come along?
If you have never tried bouldering before it is recommended that you have a guided session from the event leader, who will go through everything you will need to know to boulder safely before joining in with the rest of the group.
I've been Bouldering before?
As above, everyone attending the event  must abide by the centre's T&Cs etc. As an experienced boulderer you responsible for your own safety, actions and involvement at all times. As such, you should use your own judgement in taking up advice given either by OutdoorLads Volunteers or other experienced climbers.
Photos : Copyright granted to OutdoorLads by the event leader / venue.
Please note that every person participating should be aware of (and accept) that those members perceived as ‘in charge’ or leading the event are not necessarily qualified, do not need to be qualified, and are not regarded by OutdoorLads, or themselves as ‘technical experts’ but are amateur climbers with some experience and who are happy to impart their knowledge. Any advice given should be considered with this in mind by the recipient.
All those attending the event must also be aware of the centre's own terms and conditions of participation and abide by them, along with any instructions given by the centre’s staff. Everyone must also take responsibility for their own safety while participating in Indoor climbing events.
Difficulty & Distance: Suitable for all abilities. If you're new to bouldering, just let the event leader know and we'll get you started!

Title: Sheffield Bouldering @ The Climbing Hanger
Summary:
Swap trainers for climbing shoes and join OutdoorLads for an evening of INDOOR BOULDERING at The Climbing Hanger in Sheffield.
Cards/contactless ONLY  - this venue does not accept cash
If you haven't been to The Hangar before, please click
here
to pre-register. Click on the link, scroll down, select Sheffield and follow the on-screen instructions. Completing the waiver prior to arrival will get you climbing faster.
What is Bouldering?
It's a form of climbing where instead of long high routes, the 'problems' tend to be shorter and focus on technique, balance, body positioning and sometimes power. Because of this different emphasis, bouldering doesn't have the hassle of climbing with ropes, harnesses and belaying, which makes it an excellent activity for newcomers to the sport. No wonder it's often regarded as the 'purest' form of climbing.
If you're a seasoned climber you won't need convincing about the benefits of bouldering, but for those who've never climbed more than a staircase, the benefits are wide ranging: from the physical - improved strength, tone, balance, power and coordination; mental - problem solving, focus, concentration and body awareness; to the emotional - relaxation, a sense of achievement and a feeling that mind and body are working together perfectly ('feeling psyched'). On top of all this, bouldering tends to be a very social sport where you can hang out with mates and each have a go as you feel regardless of what level you climb at.
I'm new to Bouldering - can I come along?
If you have never tried bouldering before it is recommended that you have a guided session from the event leader, who will go through everything you will need to know to boulder safely before joining in with the rest of the group.
I've been Bouldering before?
As above, everyone attending the event  must abide by the centre's T&Cs etc. As an experienced boulderer you responsible for your own safety, actions and involvement at all times. As such, you should use your own judgement in taking up advice given either by OutdoorLads Volunteers or other experienced climbers.
Photos : Copyright granted to OutdoorLads by the event leader / venue.
Please note that every person participating should be aware of (and accept) that those members perceived as ‘in charge’ or leading the event are not necessarily qualified, do not need to be qualified, and are not regarded by OutdoorLads, or themselves as ‘technical experts’ but are amateur climbers with some experience and who are happy to impart their knowledge. Any advice given should be considered with this in mind by the recipient.
All those attending the event must also be aware of the centre's own terms and conditions of participation and abide by them, along with any instructions given by the centre’s staff. Everyone must also take responsibility for their own safety while participating in Indoor climbing events.

Title: Durham Beer Festival '26 Social
Summary:
Join Craig and OutdoorLads for our next social event in Durham.
Durham is a beautiful cathedral and university city in North-East England. It is also home to
Durham Castle
, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Bill Bryson has called Durham ‘a perfect little city’, Condé Nast’s Traveller magazine readers voted it the ‘Best city in the UK’, and Which? members named it one of the UK's best city breaks for 2024. It is also the county town of County Durham. Durham is surrounded on three sides by a meander in the River Wear, with the surrounding land being hilly, except along the Wear's floodplain to the north and southeast.
The
Durham Beer Festival
(page currently still points to last year's event) is organised by
CAMRA
. They expect to have 30+ real ales and 8+ ciders and perries, selected from a wide range of independent national brewers.
Entry last year cost £5, which also included a half-pint glass and a programme. This page will be updated when more information becomes available.
Come and join us for a few drinks, find out about planned and forthcoming events, make new friends and catch up with people you have met before!
If you’re not familiar with Durham, it’s a wonderful city to explore on foot and make sure you include the cathedral.
All images provided by the event leader. OutdoorLads has permission to use them.
Difficulty & Distance: Social event

Title: Southampton Social - Box Bar with Bingo!
Summary:
Join me at The Edge in Southampton - Southampton's Premier Award Winning LGBTQ+ Cocktail Bar & Nightclub for the July social!
There will be Drag Bingo from 9pm plus 2 for 1 Cocktails! If you'd like to take part in the bingo this costs £4 To Play - 2 Games - PLUS Bonus Bingo Round!
This is a perfect event for members old and new to catch up with each other.
https://www.theedgesouthampton.com/theboxbar
Pictures courtesy of the Edge website / Facebook account and user GeekyJames
Difficulty & Distance: Social event

Title: Hebden Bridge, Hardcastle Crags, Colden Water, Heptonstall
Summary:
Join Justin on this gem of a walk set around Hebden Bridge in the heart of the South Pennines. Geographically and visually stimulating with views a plenty!
We will take in mature wooded valleys, moorland, crags, a 13th century ruin and a beautiful hilltop stone cobbled Yorkshire village - as well as the delights that Hebden Bridge has top offer.
We will head into Hardcastle Crags, over Heptonstall Moor, skirt Colden Clough and stop at Heptonstall village. We will then finish at Hebden Bridge and one of many great pubs this town has to offer.
This is a hilly but very rewarding walk - approximately 11 miles.
Please bring appropriate clothing and footwear.
This is a dog friendly walk:
well-behaved dogs are welcome on the walk, but must be under control at all times on a lead where necessary. There are very few roads.
If the event is fully booked:
Please join the waiting list as people frequently drop out during the week before an event, and you are likely to get a place on the walk.
If this is your first OutdoorLads walk event,
we’ll make sure you feel welcome. Please
check out the My First Event page
, and don’t hesitate to drop me a message if you have questions or want to know more about this walk.
All photos taken by the event leader. OutdoorLads has permission to use them.
Difficulty & Distance: 11 miles long and there is a lot of uphill walking, but the pace is unrushed.

Title: The Tolkien Trail
Summary:
Join Mike and OutdoorLads for a 7 mile walk in the footsteps of J. R. R. Tolkien, famous author of ‘The Lord of the Rings’ trilogy and ‘The Hobbit’. We will cross farmland and some woodland and walk along the banks of the River Ribble and River Hodder. Points of interest include Stonyhurst College, where Tolkien is said to have spent a lot of time whilst writing the books, drawing inspiration from the surrounding areas. We will also pass the historic landmark, ‘Cromwell’s bridge’ on the River Hodder, named after Oliver Cromwell, who marched his army over the bridge in 1648 to fight in the Battle of Preston. This beautiful part of Lancashire is full of history, and with stunning views of Pendle Hill and the surrounding countryside, what more could you ask for?
We will stop for lunch at the midway point of our walk and, for those who wish to, there will be an opportunity to have a post-walk drink in one of the local establishments in Hurst Green.
Your First OutdoorLads Walk Event?
We know that new members in particular like to know in advance more about what an OutdoorLads event will be like.
If this is your first OutdoorLads event, you should check out the information designed specifically for you on the
My First Event
page on the OutdoorLads website.
Photo credit:
All photos taken by walk leader, Mike, OutdoorLads has permission to use them.
Difficulty & Distance: The walk is approximately 7-miles long, relatively flat on well-defined footpaths, with some uphill climbs.

Title: Barbon and the Big Boulders day walk
Summary:
Yes , it's a
Saturday
walk for a change-  how very daring!!
Join Stephen for a 9 mile walk from the picturesque village of
Barbon
near
Kirkby Lonsdale
in
Cumbria
The highlights of the walk will be -
• Walking through Barbon past the Church
• Walking through the woodlands of Barbondale
• Ascending up onto the fells above Barbon passing Bullpot Farm
• Going round Lower Barbon Fell with views down the Lune Valley, Cumbrian Fells and if its clear- out to The Irish Sea
• Dropping down from the fells and seeing the Art Installations by Andy Goldsworthy, which are big boulders enclosed in Sheepfolds
(
http://www.sheepfoldscumbria.co.uk/html/info/info00.htm
)
• Then walking through the grounds of Whelprigg Hall and back into the village
Then after head to the Barbon Inn for a post walk drink
https://www.barbon-inn.co.uk/
NB - there is no public transport to Barbon - please consider car sharing/asking for a lift.
Image / photo credits:  All leader's own images - with permission for OutdoorLads to use and reuse.
Are you new to Outdoorlads and considering your first event?
Have a look at this link, hopefully it will help.
https://www.outdoorlads.com/my-first-event
Don’t worry if the event is full.
Do sign up and join the waiting list.
Often those on the main list cancel nearer the time.
There is a very high chance you will still get a place
Difficulty & Distance: Long gradual climb for half of the walk

Title: Reading Indoor Bouldering
Summary:
This bouldering session presents an ideal opportunity for a fun evening of what I call sociable workout where we take on a wide range of physical (and in fact mental) challenges with the atmosphere always friendly and cheerful. All levels are welcome!
The beauty of bouldering lies in its simplicity: you don't need ropes or a harness, just some climbing shoes which can be hired at the centre and a drive to get the top and overcome some trepidation. It's also a fundamentally social experience, with most of the session spent discussing how to solve particular climbing routes, aka problems, or catching up with each other in between climbs. Bouldering is a great way to get into climbing, come and see what all the fuss is about!
All skill levels are welcome
. If you're new to climbing, we've got you covered with tailored guidance, boosting your skills and confidence while ensuring your safety on and around the wall. Don’t worry about needing to invest in gear before you try. The Climbing Hangar offers
free shoe rentals
so you don’t have to shell out for shoes until you know bouldering is for you.
Experienced boulderer? Dive in and embrace the challenges independently. Just remember to follow the centre's rules for your own safety.
Our ethos is inclusivity. I keep our group tight-knit to foster connection and ensure personalised coaching for everyone, regardless of your experience level. Many climbers started their journeys relatively “late” in life, incl. some session leaders
🤫
We convene at the
Climbing Hangar in Reading
, one of the largest climbing centres in the region. The generously spacious and friendly cafe/lounge is my personal favourite where you can find us socialising, having a cuppa, snacks or even pizza before and after the climbs.
If you have never climbed at the Climbing Hangar in Reading before, you must book an induction slot:
https://www.theclimbinghangar.com/locations/reading
- follow instructions to book an induction slot around the time that this event starts.
You also need to
complete the waiver form that is linked in the confirmation email
once you've booked an induction slot.
This only needs to be done once.
Want to Get Out More?
To be informed of all our North West Home Counties* events please click on this Telegram link
https://t.me/+EoB--tkEV3s4ZWJk
* Berkshire, Oxfordshire, Buckinghamshire, Hertfordshire & Bedfordshire
Photo Credits:
All photos taken by OutdoorLads Members and permission is granted to OutdoorLads for further use.
Difficulty & Distance: Difficulty can be adapted depending on experience

Title: Summer hike in The Upper Calder Valley
Summary:
This walk is a hilly 14 miles! but there are great views, wooded valleys, and water!
During this walk, we will explore the hinterland between Todmorden and Hebden Bridge.
We will call at Great Rock, Jumble Hole, Callis Bridge, Studley Pike and Gaddins Dam (England's highest beach,) before heading back to Todmorden.
This walk can be cut short depending on conditions, such as the weather!
We will finish in the Golden Lion for a great selection of beers and a Thai kitchen
Dogs:
well-behaved dogs are welcome on the walk, but must be under control at all times on a lead where necessary.
If the event is fully booked:
Please join the waiting list as people frequently drop out during the week before an event, and you are likely to get a place on the walk.
If this is your first OutdoorLads walk event,
we’ll make sure you feel welcome. Please
check out the My First Event page
, and don’t hesitate to drop me a message if you have questions or want to know more about this walk.
Image credits: all images provided by the event leader. OutdoorLads has permission to use them
Difficulty & Distance: Hilly walk. Total ascent just under 600m

Title: Oxford Social
Summary:
Hi Guys. Why not join us for a summer drink or two in the garden of  Oxford's famous gay pub - the Jolly Farmers. The event starts at 17.00 directly after the Queer Oxford day walk around the city (link
here
), so you'd be really welcome to do one or both.
The JF doesn't do food in the evening but is happy for you to order takeaways to eat in the garden. The pub is dog friendly.
All photos taken by the leader, with permission to OutdoorLads for use.

Title: Queer Oxford
Summary:
Hi guys. Join me for a city walk looking at colleges, pubs, clubs, statues and a few hidden gems of LGBTQ+ Oxford.
After meeting up at the Station we will walk to the city centre, past some of the venues from the 1980s and 90s - the Red Lion, the long-gone Stage Club, and the Northgate Hall. We'll here about some of the legendary names including British physician Michael Dillon who is documented as the first known person in the world to transition from female to male using hormone therapy, Iris Murdoch whose private diaries reveal her relationships with women, and T E Lawrence who had a lifelong emotional bond with a Syrian man he nicknamed Dahoum. We'll see 'Mesopotamia', a gay cruising area of the 1970s.
We will also visit Magdalen College (£10 entry - or free to anyone living near the city or holding University, Bodleian Library or OUP membership). Here we can grab a drink and lunch in the Old Kitchen Bar with its terrace overlooking the punters on the River Cherwell and directly under Oscar Wilde's college rooms.
For those who want to stay on, the day will end with a social at the Jolly Farmers, Oxford's legendary LGBTQ+ pub in Paradise St (please sign up for this social too - link
here
)
Dogs:
Unfortunately, dogs (other than guide dogs) are not allowed in Magdalen College.
All photos taken by the leader, with permission to OutdoorLads for use.
Difficulty & Distance: Flat walk on city pavements throughout

Title: Roundhay Park - Autumn Walk
Summary:
Join Shaun and Mike for a relaxed 6.5km (4 miles) scenic autumn walk through one of Europe’s largest urban parks. Roundhay Park is a place where Leeds’ history, landscape, and culture all meet. From lakeside views and ornamental gardens to grand 18th‑century architecture and the echoes of world‑famous concerts.
We’ll set off from within the park and make our way around, visiting the folly built in the late 18th century as a summerhouse. This charming stone structure sits tucked above the lake and offers a glimpse into the park’s Georgian past. We’ll also see the Mansion, which overlooks sweeping lawns and remains a focal point within the park.
We’ll take our time, enjoy the scenery, and make the most of the park. There will be three opportunities for refreshments during the walk, so there’s no need to bring food, although you’re very welcome to do so.
Dogs are welcome
on this walk.
If the event is fully booked:
please join the waiting list as people frequently drop out during the week before an event, and you are likely to get a place on the walk.
Your First OutdoorLads Event?
We know that new members, in particular, like to know in advance more about what an OutdoorLads event will be like. If this is your first OutdoorLads event, you should check out the information designed specifically for you on the
My First Event
page on the OutdoorLads website.
Image credits: Images are provided by event Leader. OutdoorLads has permission to use them. Also from Geograph Richard Vince, Stephen Craven and John Slater.
Difficulty & Distance: It's easy as it's a leisure walk, time to relax and socialise throughout the walk.

Title: London Ten Pin Bowling with Optional Pub Social 🎳😃
Summary:
GET SOCIAL WITH OUTDOORLADS! Great for new and regular members!
If the event is full, please
add yourself to the waitlist
, and we will amend our booking with Rowan's if availability permits.
Ten pin bowling and optional pub social which will now take place on a regular monthly basis, as the 1st event was so well received by the attendees. All are welcome on this event, whether you’ve bowled before or you’re a complete beginner. If you’ve never bowled before come along and have some Fun! I’m happy to show/demonstrate how I bowl. Pretty average!?
Come join me and the other guys for an evening of ten pin bowling fun in north London.  We have a group booking for 19.00 Wednesday night at Rowan's Ten Pin Bowl in Finsbury Park. All Welcome! Please arrive and check in by 18.45 so we have time to get all set up! We have two games per person booked.
PLEASE NOTE:- There is an admission fee to Rowan’s (Not included in the event charge). This is payable upon entry.
FRIDAY 10:30am - 7:00pm  £1.00
7:00pm - Close  £5.00
THE T&C’S OF ROWAN’S ALSO REQUIRE PHOTO ID ON FRIDAY & SATURDAY EVENINGS. (Obviously probably dependent on what age you appear to be).
You DO NOT need to book with Rowan's. I will make the booking prior to event once appropriate attendee numbers are known.
The cost is £21.60 (Fridays) per player for 2 games of ten pin bowling.
Difficulty & Distance: Bowling - very easy!

Title: Evening MTB/Gravel around Burbage & Stanage Rocks
Summary:
Take advantage of the long Summer Evening, come ride a 22km route on the outskirts of Sheffield.
We start with a trail over to Ringinglow, followed with a road section around the Redmires Reservoirs.  before the highlight of the routes with an offroad section up to Stanage Pole before passing Stanage Rocks.
A further road section climbs up to Burbage Bridge before finishing with a great descent below Burbage Rocks
For this ride you will need a reasonable quality  mountain bike, or gravel bike as nothing technical on these trails. However a reasonable level of fitness is required, Bring lights in case of any unforeseen delays
Image credits:
All photos taken by the leader and permission is granted to ODL for their use.
Difficulty & Distance: 22km with 450m ascent

Title: BIRMINGHAM Indoor Climbing - MONDAY - Experienced
Summary:
Join OutdoorLads for an evening at Redpoint Birmingham Climbing Centre.
"EXPERIENCED" in this instance is those that have previously climbed & can answer YES to all three of these questions:
Can you fit a climbing harness correctly?
Can you tie in to a rope using the correct knots?
Can you belay another climber correctly & confidently?
Please ensure you only sign up for this event if you
satisfy all three of the prerequisites as an experienced (independent) climber!
Never climbed before? Need a refresher on the basics? Answered NO to any of the three questions above? You will need to
attend one of the beginners (supervised) events
instead, where we will happily sign you in to be supervised (two people per leader maximum), show you the ropes & teach you everything you need to know to climb safely! Beginner (supervised) events are not offered every week.
If you are climbing with us for the first time, whether Experienced or Beginner, it is essential that you send a note to the Event Leader telling them about your previous climbing experience & your goals!
Bouldering
- Most of us do roped climbing, but if you prefer to do bouldering, you can sign yourself in & go bouldering (unsupervised) instead of, or as well as, roped climbing. Please meet the leader & the group at the start though; some others might wish to do bouldering too!
Please note that we will not teach lead climbing or lead belaying on this event -
we would suggest having some
lessons in Lead Climbing
by a qualified member of staff if this is something you're interested in pursuing!
If you are subsequently unable to attend, please cancel off the event as 'no-shows' are not only rude, but also frustrating for the leader & other attendees who may have travelled over an hour away to climb with you!
Redpoint Birmingham Climbing Centre
Indoor climbing centres offer the challenges of climbing that you'll find on natural rock crags - simply indoors! It's a fun way to stay fit, make new friends & develop your climbing skills.
Climbing
- Unlike Indoor Bouldering there is a certain amount of skill to be mastered, such as tying in knots & belaying a partner, as well as the climbs being higher than in bouldering. If you are new to climbing or would like a refresher, then this is just for you!! If you’ve climbed before, can safely tie into a harness & can competently belay, then there’s plenty of choices of top-rope & lead climbing on offer here.
Bouldering
- Instead of tackling long high routes, the 'problems' (as they are referred to) are shorter & focus on technique, balance, body positioning & sometimes power. With the different emphasis, bouldering doesn't have the hassle of climbing with ropes, so it’s excellent (but not necessarily easier) for newcomers.
Redpoint Birmingham Climbing Centre
has more than 75 lines with over 300 routes, set through some spectacular ground including its notorious 'stalactite'. It even features one of the few indoor 'Yorkshire VS cracks' in the country, to test your jamming skills. The bouldering rooms are just as interesting as its climbing routes, over 200 bouldering problems have been organised into graded circuits to provide something for everyone.
You MUST register via this event page before coming to the centre!

(please arrive early to do this in-person the first time) & pay a Registration Fee:

is

or

is

Entry Fee
directly to the climbing centre:

is

&

is
£8.50.
Hire
climbing shoes (
£3.50
) & a climbing harness (
£3.50
) if you don't own your own.
Please see Redpoint's
website
for current prices & note that prices are subject to change.
If you have any questions or need any more information just get in touch with the event leader by clicking on their profile below, or take a look at the
My first climbing event page
for more information.
PLEASE NOTE:
All those attending the event must also be aware of the climbing centre's own terms & conditions of participation & abide by them, along with any instructions given by the centre's staff.

taken & used with permission of
R. Casabuena
&

.

Title: Everybody's talking about Jamie
Summary:
Following a record-breaking three-year West End residency, two sold-out UK & Ireland Tours and Amazon Studios award-winning film, the smash-hit and critically acclaimed musical
Everybody’s Talking About Jamie
returns for the
10th Anniversary Tour
with a brand-new song.
Jamie New is sixteen (
Ashcon Rahmani
) and lives on a council estate in Sheffield. Jamie doesn’t quite fit in. Jamie is terrified about the future. Jamie is going to be a sensation.
Supported by his brilliant loving mum (
Rebecca Trehearn
) and surrounded by his friends, Jamie overcomes prejudice, beats the bullies and steps out of the darkness, into the spotlight. This ‘Funny, outrageous, touching’ (
Daily Telegraph
), musical sensation is to be experienced by all the family and not to be missed!
With an original score of catchy pop tunes that will ‘blow the roof off the Theatre’ (
Mail on Sunday
) by lead singer-songwriter of The Feeling,
Dan Gillespie Sells
and writer
Tom MacRae
(
Doctor Who
), this ‘Sparkling coming-of-age musical’ (
The Times
) will have everybody talking about Jamie for years to come.
Sixteen: the edge of possibility. Time to make your dreams come true.
If you would like to sit near the rest of the group, the leader will be seated in stalls row H.
We may go to a nearby bar for a couple of drinks after the performance.
Image credits: Main banner image provided by event leader; Other images free to use from pixels.com - Iryana Varonovich and cottonbro studios
Difficulty & Distance: Social event

Title: Yorkshire Dales Camp 2026
Summary:
Join OutdoorLads for a weekend in the beautiful Yorkshire Dales National Park in late Summer!
We will be camping in Upper Wharfedale, at the foot of Great Whernside.  Our location provides stunning views across the valley and opportunity for varied activities, including
walking, mountain biking and road cycling
. The main event on the Saturday will be a hike with wonderful views across the landscape - so it will involve a good climb! There may also be an easier low-level walk (subject to leader availability). On the Sunday there will most likely be a shorter and lower level walk on offer.
Our campsite is close to
Kettlewell Village
which has a range of facilities including three great pubs and an award-winning tearoom. There's even a traditional village store and an outdoor shop in case you forget that essential item. The campsite itself will be for the
exclusive use of OutdoorLads
, and has its own dedicated facilities, including free high quality hot showers, picnic benches and fire pit. The campsite allows BBQ'S but campfires that are ground based are not permitted. Fires raised off the floor and with protection for the grass underneath are permitted. There are a limited number of electric-hookups available which will be allocated on a first-come first served basis, if you want one of these then message the leader following event registration.
This is a compact site and we would encourage you to bring a smaller rather than a big party tent! You will not be able to park your car immediately next to your tent, but there should be room on site if people try to car share as far as possible.
Dogs:
Well behaved dogs are allowed but must be on the lead on the site, due to the proximity of livestock.
Caravans, Campervans, Motorhomes
: This site is not suitable for these vehicles due to the access. Very small campervans that no larger than a car, however, are allowed.
Photos:
By the event leaders and used with permission.
Difficulty & Distance: The dales have hills everywhere, but this is not technical walking

Title: Porter Valley Mid-Week Evening Walk
Summary:
Join Tim for a relaxed four mille stroll through Endcliffe and Bingham Parks and the Porter Valley. Our route follows the Porter Brook from Endciffe Park to Whitely Woods and Forge Dam, looping back via the Porter Valley Woodland Nature Reserve. Mosty tarmac and easy woodland tracks. We will go to one of the local pubs for a pint afterwards.
If the event is fully booked:
Please join the wait list as people frequently drop out during the week before an event and you are likely to get a place on the walk.
If this is your first OutdoorLads walk event:
If this is your first event, we’ll make sure you feel welcomed. Please
check out the My First Event page
, and don’t hesitate to drop me a message if you have questions or want to know more about this walk.
image credits -
all images provided by the event leader. OutdoorLads has permission to use them.
Difficulty & Distance: 4 mile walk on easy terrain.

Title: Spa Valley Railway Beer Festival & Woodland Walk
Summary:
All Aboard for Ales, Ciders, Steam Trains & Live Music!
Fancy a day out combining beautiful countryside, heritage steam trains and one of Kent's biggest beer festivals?
Join us for a leisurely
5-mile woodland walk
from Tunbridge Wells through the historic Common and along part of the Tunbridge Wells Circular route. Along the way we'll take in the spectacular
High Rocks
before arriving at
Groombridge
.
Once there, enjoy the renowned
Spa Valley Railway Beer Festival
, featuring real ales, ciders, traditional Kent Green Hop beers, live music and heritage steam trains.
The best part? You can enjoy a pint while exploring the platforms, engine sheds and travelling aboard historic steam trains throughout the afternoon and evening.
🍻 Festival Highlights
Over
230 real ales
Dozens of ciders and Perry
Traditional Kent Green Hop beers
Live bands throughout the day
Heritage steam train rides
Beer available across platforms, trains and engine sheds
🥾 Woodland Walk
Route:
Start: Tunbridge Wells Station
Tunbridge Wells Common
High Rocks
Finish: Groombridge Station & Beer Festival
Terrain:
Largely flat, some gentle climbs, possible muddy sections
Distance:
Approximately 5.5 miles
A relaxed-paced walk suitable for most fitness levels.
🚆 Getting There
By Train
Tunbridge Wells Station
(Southeastern)
London Bridge → Tunbridge Wells
Brighton → Tunbridge Wells (approximately 3 changes)
By Bus
Regency Route 29
from Brighton

🎟️ Tickets
Steam Railway
Adult Unlimited Day Rover Ticket:
£20.00
Beer Festival
Entry to the festival is
free
, with drinks purchased separately.
CAMRA Members
🍺 Receive
25% off railway tickets
at the ticket office with a valid CAMRA membership card.
For the latest information and ticket prices, visit the
Spa Valley Railway website
.
🏨 Accommodation
For those wishing to stay overnight, there are plenty of accommodation options available.
Recommended:
Travelodge Tunbridge Wells (where i will be staying).
New Members
If this is your first flight or you are new to OutdoorLads passenger we’d love to see you. This type of event is perfect for you to get to know other ODL members. Please message me if you have any questions.
Photo credits martiebear free for outdoorlads to use
Difficulty & Distance: 5.5 mile  generally easy to moderate

Title: Bouldering MANCHESTER - Beginners welcome
Summary:
Join OutdoorLads For An Evening of INDOOR BOULDERING in Manchester!
This event is intended as a safe space for the LGBTQ+ community; everyone will be warmly welcomed
Bouldering is a form of climbing that focuses on shorter "problems" emphasising technique, balance, body positioning, and sometimes power, rather than long, high routes. Without the need for ropes, harnesses, and belaying, bouldering is an accessible activity for newcomers and is often considered the "purest" form of climbing.
Bouldering offers a wide range of benefits, including:
Physical:
Improved strength, tone, balance, power, and coordination.
Mental:
Enhanced problem-solving, focus, concentration, and body awareness.
Emotional:
Relaxation, a sense of achievement, and a feeling of mind-body connection.
Bouldering is also a fantastic way to meet new people and build lasting friendships, as it's an inherently social sport where climbers share tips and strategies to conquer the routes.
New to Bouldering?
If you're new to bouldering, it is recommended that you have a guided session from the event leader to learn the basics of safe bouldering. The leader can then introduce you to the rest of the group where you can refine your newly learnt skills in a social environment.
Experienced Boulderer?
If you’re new to climbing with ODL, the leader will help to introduce you to the rest of the group so you can explore the centre together.
You are responsible for your own safety and actions while bouldering. Please use your own judgement when accepting advice from OutdoorLads Volunteers or other experienced climbers, regardless of your bouldering experience.
Ready to boulder?
You will need to sign up on the ODL event page. We would ask you to do this each time so that the leader has access to important information
If you are not already registered with Rock Over Climbing, please do so
here
to save time when you arrive. You only need to do this once.
When you arrive at the centre you will need to pay for entry and hire climbing shoes if you don't own a pair. ODL members benefit from the concessionary rate so please mention this when paying.
Get bouldering! You’ll find most of the group already bouldering so feel free to join them. If you’re new to bouldering with ODL, the leader will be looking out for you so if you spot them, please go speak with them first.
Any questions?
If you have any questions, please contact the leader directly.
Photos : Copyright granted to OutdoorLads by
Sieh
and joenellist.
Difficulty & Distance: Suitable for all abilities. If you're new to bouldering, just let the event leader know and we'll get you started!

Title: Indoor Climbing MANCHESTER - Beginners welcome!
Summary:
This event is intended as a safe space for the LGBTQ+ community; everyone will be warmly welcomed
Join us for an evening of climbing in one of the UK's top climbing centres. Whether you are new to climbing or a seasoned climber, this centre offers something for everyone with a huge selection of routes including top rope, lead and auto belay.
Prefer to boulder? That's well catered for as well, with an extensive modern bouldering area ensuring there is plenty to keep you busy, or you can swap between climbing and bouldering on the same night.
Climbing is the main focus of the event, but this is also a great chance to meet other members and build lasting friendships. Many Outdoor Lads members join through climbing, so if you're new to the group and want to know more, come along and chat over a cuppa during the tea and cake break.
What to expect on your first climb with us?
If you are an experienced climber and have passed the centre's proficiency test, we'll pair you up with a climbing buddy and you'll be out exploring the centre in no time.
If you have never climbed before or think you could do with a refresher, we offer supervised climbing sessions with one of our leaders, subject to availability. That session will focus on introducing you to climbing, making sure you get plenty of time on the wall whilst also showing you how to belay safely. Due to the centre's rules, we are limited in the number of climbers the leader is able to supervise in each session so if you think you may need a supervised session,
please contact the leader before signing up to make sure there is enough space.
As a general guide, if you answer "no" to any of the following questions, you should opt for a supervised session:
Can you fit a climbing harness correctly?
Can you tie into a rope using the correct knots?
Can you belay and lower down another climber correctly and confidently?
After a few supervised sessions, you should be ready to complete the centre's proficiency test and once that has been passed, you'll be able to climb with the rest of the group.
Everything else you need to know
You will need to register with Big Depot before your first visit to the centre but you only need to do this once. If you are an experienced climber, you can save time and do this online
here
, selecting the 18+ unsupervised option. This also applies if you just want to boulder, just select the relevant options during the registration. If you are a beginner and require a supervised session, the leader will help register you when you are at the centre.
Photos provided by Sieh.
Difficulty & Distance: There is a range of routes of varying difficulty, but there should be something to suit everybody

Title: Ballantrae to Girvan - Ayrshire Coastal Path
Summary:
Ayrshire Coastal Path
A stunning walk with coastal views. From Ballantrae harbour along to Bennane Hill with a short de-tour to visit Snib's cave. We finish up with an enjoyable walk along Lendalfoot's sandy beach to finish at the Varyag Memorial.
On a good weather day you simply cannot beat the views from Pinbain Hill, which you access via an 18th century coach road taking you up and over the historic Kennedy's Pass. Keep your eyes peeled for Roe Deer and Foxes!
Photos:
gillianswalks.com with permission

groups, and bookmark our upcoming events list via our

.

Title: Maidens & Culzean to Dunure - Ayrshire Coastal Path
Summary:
This is an easy walk between the harbours of Maidens and Dunure. Terrain includes firm sandy beaches, woodland earth paths and grassy farm tracks. Enjoy the clifftop views of the more rugged sections of coastline around Culzean Castle & Country Park and the fields just south of Dunure. You will even pass directly in front of the incredible 18th century
Culzean Castle
, as well as the remains of
Dunure Castle
– which dates back as far as the 12th century.

Title: Hull Social October 2026
Summary:
Join us as we return to one of the National Geographic "Top 10 places to visit in 2026"
Hull is rich in culture, with stunning museums; art galleries; cream phone boxes; Europe's largest 'submarium' (complete with Sharks); old bars that played host to the beginning of the English Civil War and the backdrop for many TV and film productions (Victoria, The Crown, A Royal Night out, Enola Holmes).  The city also has a buzzing gay scene in the newest Freedom Quarter, which hosts multiple gay bars and clubs.
We will be organising several other events in the area over the weekend, which will be shared nearer the time.
Although the event officially ends at 10pm, it is likely that some of us will end up in a late night bar, such as Broadway show bar or firebox (usually open until 6am).
This is a fantastic opportunity to meet new people or catch up with existing friends. All are welcome.
Images courtesy of unsplash.com, a free image resource area.

Title: Creigau Gleision & Cwm Glas Crafnant
Summary:
Join OutdoorLads for some epic hill walking though some more remote & wild mountains in Wales, with rocky peaks, glistening lakes and panoramic views.
Points of interest on the route:
Cwm Glas Crafnant NNR:
This beautiful valley is a mix of woodland and open upland grassland. It features upland ashwood and oakwood alongside areas of grassland, some of which are fenced off to protect from grazing. The reserve is home to a variety of resident birds, rare moths, native reptiles and the local Carneddau ponies.
Creigiau Gleision:
At 678m, our first summit is an eastern ridge of the Carneddau mountain range, primarily composed of volcanic and intrusive igneous rocks. The name, which translates to "grey-green rocks," reflects the color and composition of the craggy, heather-covered terrain. The ridge is an impressive example of upland habitat, and the surrounding area has been designated as a Special Area of Conservation due to its geology
Llyn Cowlyd Reservoir:
This is the deepest lake in North Wales. While it was originally a natural glacial lake, it is now a reservoir that supplies water to nearby towns, with its depth having been increased by a dam
Crimpiau:
At 475m, our smaller second peak offers unexpectedly magnificent panoramic views of the main Snowdonia mountain ranges, including the Snowdon Horseshoe and the Glyderau. It features rugged, volcanic terrain carved by ice and water, which results in both solid rocky paths and naturally boggy sections, particularly in the lower areas.
Other Information:
Dog Suitability:
Dogs are always welcome on my walks, though their behaviour and safety is their owner's responsibility. This includes cleaning up after them, controlling their behaviour around other animals or people, and assisting them over any stiles or streams we may cross.
New Members
We know that new members in particular like to know in advance more about what an OutdoorLads event will be like.
If this is your first OutdoorLads event, you should check out the information designed specifically for you on the
My First Event
page on the OutdoorLads website.
What if there is a Waiting List?
If this event is fully booked and you want to attend, just add yourself to the waitlist, because places inevitably become available closer to the date.
All photos taken by the event leader, with permission given for further use by OutdoorLads.
Difficulty & Distance: This hike is about 13km (8 miles) with ~600m (2000ft) of ascent. While there's nothing overly difficult on this route, there are some steep sections and it may be wet and muddy if there's been rainfall.

Title: Glyder Fach & Glyder Fawr via Bristly Ridge (Scramble)
Summary:
Fancy a BIG day out in the mountains of Snowdonia / Eryri?
The Glyderau range sits just north of the Llanberis pass and the Snowdon range, and with summits similar in height to the mighty Snowdon / Yr Wyddfa it forms a worthy, interesting and far less crowded mountain experience. Our route up will take in one of the best
grade 1 scrambles
in the area -
Bristly Ridge
, a thrilling journey ‘off-path’ route which starts in the forebodingly named
Sinister Gully
and leads up onto the pinnacles that form the upper part of this fabulous 3 starred route.
Bristly Ridge is
challenging and exposed
in places, and although you don’t need technical rock climbing skills you will definitely need a head for heights, be sure footed and have
a sense of adventure
. This event is best suited to members who have scrambled confidently before, however if you are 'good' on your feet, physically fit with a head for heights and want to give it a go, I will be happy to have you join the team.
Having championed the challenge of the ridge we will then get to explore the summit of
Glyder Fawr
and visit the improbable rock monolith of the
Cantilever Stone
,  before heading over to
Castell y Gwynt
(Castle of the Winds). The top of the mountain is fractured into a myriad of angled lichenous covered slabs which make traversing the summit challenging fun in the dry and requiring carefully foot placement in the wet.  We will then head over to the summit of Glyder Fawr, which stands at a height of 1001 meters, before descending via
Devils Kitchen
to return to our start point.
Please note that the event may be liable to change or cancellation due to inclement weather.
Event full?
Please join the waiting list. Places usually open up as the event gets nearer and, where necessary, we try to add additional leaders to increase the numbers, so you are highly likely to get a place.
Image
/
photo
credits
:  All images (c)
Andy
with permission for OutdoorLads to use and reuse.
Difficulty & Distance: Distance: 8.6km Ascent: 900 meters Time: 5 - 6 hours Difficulty: Hard Maximum attendance: 5
