Title: Bannau Brycheiniog Autumn Escape: The Brecon Peaks
Summary:
Join us for a fantastic autumn weekend away in the heart of the
Bannau Brycheiniog (Brecon Beacons)
We’ll be staying at a beautiful converted farmhouse surrounded by wooded slopes, rushing rivers, and dramatic peaks.
Whether you’re looking to conquer some serious mountain summits or enjoy a scenic, relaxed stroll along historic waterways, this weekend has something for everyone.
The hostel is a charmingly converted traditional Welsh farmhouse steeped in rustic character. It perfectly balances a remote, "getaway" vibe with modern comfort.
Historic Character:
The hostel retains its gorgeous original stonework, exposed timber beams, and a cozy lounge complete with a
wood-burning stove
—the ultimate spot to swap stories and unwind with the guys after a day on the trails.
Stunning Natural Setting:
Tucked away in a secluded valley just below the
Talybont Reservoir
, the hostel is surrounded by lush wooded slopes, cascading waterfalls, and rushing streams. You literally step out of the front door and directly onto the trails.
Dark Skies Oasis:
The area surrounding the hostel is a designated
International Dark Sky Reserve
. If the night is clear, you can take a five-minute stroll to the edge of the reservoir for breathtaking, unpolluted views of the constellations and the moon reflecting on the water.
The Weekend Plan
Friday: The Social Kick-off
Arrive from 4:30 PM and settle into our mountain home. We’ll kick things off with our traditional welcome buffet—a great chance to meet everyone and plot our adventures.
Note: Please bring a small contribution to share (enough for 2-3 people). Let’s keep it varied—creative salads, local meats, or snacks are great, but maybe let’s go easy on the quiche!
Saturday: The Main Event
We have two brilliant options for Saturday's walk, which will be finalised closer to the date depending on weather and group preference:
Option A: The Mountain High
– A classic, invigorating hike into the nearby Brecon Beacons peaks. Expect breathtaking ridges, stunning vistas, and a good workout.
Option B: The Canal Circuit
– A gentler but incredibly scenic circular walk tracking the historic Monmouthshire & Brecon Canal between Llangynidr and Talybont-on-Usk. Perfect for autumn colors, locks, and easy-going socializing.
Sunday: Morning Stretch & Depart
After breakfast, we'll pack up and check out. For those not rushing off, we can head out for a short local stroll or a pub lunch nearby before saying our goodbyes.
Food & Fuel
We’ve got you covered so you can focus on the views:
Included:
Breakfasts on Saturday and Sunday, plus a delicious group dinner on Saturday night.
Tea, Coffee & Cake:
Plenty provided throughout the weekend—because every hiker needs a sugar hit!
Your Part:
Just bring your Friday buffet contribution, your own preferred snacks/drinks, and a packed lunch for Saturday.
Dogs:
Sorry no dogs allow for this weekend (except registered assistance dogs - please let the office know
support@outdoorlads.com
when booking)

Title: August Bank Holiday- 3 night hostel in the Yorkshire Dales
Summary:
EARLY BIRDS! The first 3 members to book will save 10% at the checkout! 👛
"The charm of Kettlewell is an abiding charm, and to those of us whose life is spent amid the hurley-burley of city life, the village seems the peculiar abode of peace and quiet beauty; its limestone terraces, with their fringes of hazel and rowan coppices, give to the district a characteristic beauty." (Professor Moorman)
Don't want to waste the last bank holiday before Christmas?
Want to get away from the hurley-burley of city life?
Just want to relax and recharge?
We have just what you need - a
3 night
hostel weekend in the Yorkshire Dales, staying in a spacious independent hostel, right in the heart of the village. The village is a stunning, picture-box classic Yorkshire Dales village, with stone house, a stream running through it, a proper old-fashioned village shop and three utterly superb public houses. Oh and absolutely stunning views in every direction. Kettlewell will not disappoint you!
On Saturday, there will be a led full day dales walk, and on Sunday, as we have a bonus day, we will be doing some activities based around the village (more details will be provided at the welcome meeting)
There's a good size lounge and separate dining room, cosy conservatory area and a large kitchen together with bike store and a range of bedroom sizes. Outside, there is a large garden with beautiful views in every direction. There is no TV and Wi-Fi is not brilliant, so a chance to socialise instead!
Note:
Bedding is provided. You may wish to bring your own pillow from home.
Dogs:
There are two well-behaved dogs booked to attend. Two is the maximum allowed so there are no more dog spaces available.
Photos:
Provided by the event leader and used by OutdoorLads with permission.
Difficulty & Distance: Hostel Event: Easy.  Walks: Moderate, and will involve some uneven ground

Title: Family Christmas in the Yorkshire Dales
Summary:
A Christmas of memories with your OutdoorLads family.
We wish you a Family Christmas, we wish you a Family Christmas…
so come spend it with us in the Yorkshire Dales.
Family Christmas returns to Yorkshire, in one of the most welcoming spots we know! Join Chris and Mike for a festive three-nights in the Yorkshire Dales.
Whether you’re looking for a change from your usual plans or simply want somewhere to belong this year, we’ll be here to cook for you, laugh with you, and keep the festive spirit flowing. Expect chilled evenings in the lounge, maybe a movie night, a cheeky dance, terrible cracker jokes, and plenty of food.
With the Dales right outside the door, we’ll stretch our legs on Christmas Day and take a longer walk on Boxing Day, though you’re just as welcome to stay cosy inside with board games, chats, or a well‑earned nap.
Cheesy jumpers encouraged, Secret Santa guaranteed.
It’s the perfect way to spend the season — together, relaxed, and surrounded by the beauty of the Dales.
Accommodation
Our venue is a spacious independent hostel with a good size lounge and separate dining room, cosy conservatory area and a large kitchen together with bike store and a range of bedroom sizes. Outside, there's a large garden with beautiful views in every direction. If the weather is good there's a firepit, and a chance to relax around a fire. If not don't worry, we have the cosy hostel for comfort, relaxing chatting, board games etc.
There is no TV and while there is WiFi it can be patchy, so this hostel is a chance to socialise away from our screens!
There's a small but well stocked village shop a short walk away and provides all you may need, and there's a choice of excellent pubs in the village serving home cooked food (depending upon their Christmas opening times).
Food
Christmas Eve will be a buffet - bring an item for a one or two people to share but don't bring too much; we like to avoid too much wastage of food wherever possible. Dinner on Christmas day and Boxing day will be a shared meal cooked by the leaders, with breakfasts on all mornings included.
If this is your first OutdoorLads event:
If this is your first event, we’ll make sure you feel welcomed. Please
check out the My First Hostel page
, and don’t hesitate to drop me a message if you have questions.
OUTDOORLADS FOUNDATION
Fancy coming along but having a difficult time of it (for whatever reason), and/or finding it hard to justify the expense? Apply for a place on this event through the OutdoorLads Foundation! Nobody apart from staff members will know and we don't need lots of financial info from you either. To apply through the Foundation, you'll need to complete a very short form, which can be found along with more information
here
Pay in instalments:
If you would prefer to pay in monthly instalments, please
contact the office team
(or
raise a support ticket
) and we can discuss the options available to you, depending on the time available between booking and the event taking place. There will be additional terms and conditions to agree to before you commit. You won't pay anything extra to pay in instalments and it must be fully paid one month before the event starts.
Dogs:
Registered assistance dogs are welcome, but you will need to let us know when you book on
support@outdoorlads.com
due to restrictions with the hostel.
Image / photo credits:
All images are those of OutdoorLads leaders - with permission for OutdoorLads to use. First image modified by Microsoft Copilot.
Difficulty & Distance: The weekend is as challenging as you choose to make it. Join a longer walk for a full day in the hills, other leaders present may offer alternative routes to suit different abilities. Prefer something gentler? Take a shorter wander or relax instead.

Title: BIG Pride Camp 2026
Summary:
Join us for a BIG Brighton Pride Weekend 🏳️‍🌈🏳️‍⚧️
This is the perfect event for those that would love a great day out at Pride with a great bunch of people, and be able to return to a party with the same great people without having to navigate over-crowded bars and busy festival environments.
Brighton & Hove Pride celebrates its
35th Anniversary
on over the weekend, under the theme "The Power of Love." As the UK’s biggest Pride celebration, the city will be transformed by the spectacular Community Parade on Saturday morning, which winds from Hove Lawns through the city centre in a vibrant display of solidarity and protest.
Throughout the day, the streets of Brighton come alive with a unique, festive energy as thousands gather to honour the history of the LGBTQ+ movement. With the daytime celebrations drawing crowds from across the country, the city is the place to be for those looking to soak up the atmosphere.
Saturday
The main event on Saturday is of course Brighton Pride! 🏳️‍🌈 We're not far from the train station from where you can catch a train for a short journey into Brighton to celebrate Pride 🏳️‍⚧️.  If you want to join us on the Pride Parade through the streets of Brighton please sign up
here
.
Some of our Leaders will organise groups to head in, so we're not all cramming on to the same train.
We'll also have a small selection of other events, like local walks, for those less interested in Brighton Pride. Watch this space!
Evenings
Friday evening will have the usual social buzz as people will arrive into the evening. It's always nice to catch up in the clubhouse on the first night with people we haven't seen in a while, and meet new faces. We'll have a pub quiz on Friday for those that enjoy a little competitiveness!
Saturday night is disco night! After a full day at Brighton Pride, continue the party back in the clubhouse, where theme will be... PRIDE! 🪩🏳️‍🌈💃🏳️‍⚧️🕺🪩
Sunday night, as an optional extra night, will be a quiet affair as not everybody will choose to stay. Think board games, chill vibes, and maybe a pub quiz if there's appetite (and a host).
The bar will be open each night serving a full range of drinks, and at reasonable prices, too.
Rent-a-Tent  / Campervans, Caravans, Motorhomes
Need to borrow a tent? We'll have a small number we can rent out for the weekend.
You can rent-a-tent on this page here.
If you'd like to bring a small caravan, motorhome or campervan
,
we will
ask you to reserve a spot, here
. If there is more than one person staying in it, you each need to book a place on the main event, but only one booking for the caravan / campervan needs to be made. Please note that as this venue is not ordinarily a campsite, these are not serviced pitches, and there's no chemical waste disposal on-site - it all needs to go home with you.
Food
Meals and snacks will be served each day from the clubhouse. No need to pre-order these, just order and pay at the food counter beside the bar! Breakfasts, snacks and evening meals will be available.
You are obviously also welcome to take care of your own food by your tent, but there are
strictly no BBQs on or over the grass
(this is a rugby club first and foremost). If you're hoping to BBQ over the weekend, please only do this on a hardstanding area. Camp fires are not permitted on site at all.
Make it three nights - upgrade your Sunday!
As standard, the event is Friday and Saturday night only. However, for no extra cost you are welcome to stay on site until Monday, allowing you to make more of the area, relax after the fun of Brighton and our Pride night - and maybe partake in an epic Sunday roast dinner from the clubhouse! If there is demand, we'll put on some walks or other activities on Sunday, but there wont be a disco or other entertainment that evening - it'll be more a chatting and boardgames vibe. To give us an idea of numbers, if you are likely to stay for Sunday night, please sign up to the event page you are reading now,
as well as this page here
.
Campsite Information
The camp is at a rugby club just a couple of miles from the beach, and easily accessible by rail and road. Approx one mile from the nearest train station.
Camp Facilities
Licensed Bar
Food served every day
Hot showers
Toilets & wash rooms
Changing areas
Washing up area
Drinking water taps
Grass camping field & hardstanding caravan / campervan / motorhome area
Floodlit camping area on Friday evening for late arrivals
Camp Rules
No glass policy. This is a rugby club with pitches - this rule will be strictly enforced.
OutdoorLads has a strict zero tolerance approach to drugs. Anyone found with drugs, suspected of having taken drugs, or offering drugs will be asked to leave the site immediately, and without refund. This includes prescription drugs, other than those you have been prescribed for your own use (which are fine!)
You must respect other campers and their property.
Quiet camping between 23:00 and 07:00
- there are designated areas to socialise and talk away from peoples' tents.
Please pitch tents at least 6 feet / 2 metres apart. Any closer and it represents a fire risk.
Campervans, caravans or motorhomes only permitted if you have a booked and confirmed a campervan / caravan / motorhome place (available separately at additional cost) No awnings allowed. Maximum of 15 campervans / caravans / motorhomes, all of which must be on the designated hard standing area. You will NOT be able to arrive in such a vehicle and park it in the car park. Note - these rules are imposed by the venue.
No dogs on site - this is a rugby club with pitches. This will be strictly enforced.
No open fires or BBQs permitted on site.
You are responsible for keeping your camping area clean and tidy - please ensure it is left in the state you found it when you leave.
All photos used with permission from OutdoorLads Members.
Difficulty & Distance: Relax and chill or #GetOutMore and have an action-packed weekend!

Title: River Tees evening stroll Rescheduled New Date
Summary:
Join Steven and the OutdoorLads for an easy early evening wander along the riverbank visiting the pretty Georgian town of Yarm on the south bank of the Tees in the historic county of North Yorkshire.
The route takes in part of the Teesdale Way and wanders uphill to the village of Egglescliffe on the historic County Durham side.
This walk will be done at a slower relaxed pace with several stops along the way, a few points of historic note will be highlighted by the walk leader who lives locally and is very familiar with this part of the Tees Valley.
The gradients are easy and this is an ideal walk as an introduction to the group.  There are good wide reaching  views across the river and to a greater distant prospect over to the south towards the Cleveland Hills and further to the east to the Eston Hills above Middlesbrough.
So please come along to a shorter walk in pleasant surroundings along the River Tees a great way to spend a summer evening.
We are likely to gather in one of the many cafes or public houses in the area for a swift half ,tea or coffee after. If the weather is dry and warm there are outside areas and beer gardens.
Photo Credits: All photographs were taken by the walk leader Steven and permission has been given for OutdoorLads to use them.
Difficulty & Distance: Easy introduction walk to the area 6.75km, 4.2 miles good paths

Title: Late Summer at Penpont 2026
Summary:
Join one of OutdoorLads' most popular events  - camping in the walled gardens of a stately home, beside the meandering River Usk set at the foot of the Brecon Beacons.
Many of you will know and love Penpont but for those who don't it truly is a magical place.
We will be running
hiking and canoeing,
on this event as well as some beautiful
day walks
.
Please sign up for canoeing
Here
We will have exclusive use of the amazing penpont campsite set in the gardens of the penpont estate.
If you're feeling adventurous, you will have the opportunity to explore the Brecon Beacons with some of the finest views that South Wales has to offer. If you'd like a more sedate weekend, we have plenty of less strenuous activities planned and we'll be within easy travelling distance of the towns of Brecon and Abergavenny. Alternatively you can spend the weekend relaxing within the Penpont Estate: exploring the remarkable buildings, gardens, the woodland art, hedge maze, tennis courts, croquet lawns, dower house and the 2000 acre estate that surrounds you!
The overall capacity of this event will be 40; however some places have temporarily been reserved as we need the event leaders to book on, so that we can run all the daytime activities! The capacity will be reset in the next couple of weeks.
Campervans
: Small campervans are welcome at the site, but limited to two in number and so subject to availability. They must be pre-booked with the OutdoorLads office on
support@outdoorlads.com
and there will be an additional charge. Unfortunately this site does not accept motorhomes or caravans.
Dogs
: Penpont also welcomes well behaved quiet dogs, but they must be kept under control and with you during your stay on site. There is an additional charge of £6 per dog for the weekend, to be paid in cash on arrival.
Images
: Image 1 used with permission of Penpont Estate. All other images taken by OutdoorLads members and permission is given for their use.
Difficulty & Distance: Enjoy our more streneous activities or just take it easy for the weekend.

Title: Penpont Canoeing 2026 - Glasbury to Whitney
Summary:
This is a great canoe trip down the river Wye.
The canoeing is for a full day and will
cost £45
paid to the canoe centre on the day.
Please note by signing up to this event you are committing to paying the fee (as we need to commit to the canoe centre)
. If you do not turn up you may be asked to pay for your canoe anyway!
If this event is full but you are camping at Penpont and you want to come then
please sign up on the reserve list
we may be able to increase numbers for penpont attendees.
This event is aimed at those attending the Penpont camp event. If you are not attending the camp but would like to come please sign up and confirm your attendance by messaging the event leader a week prior to the event date. (A place may not be held for you if you have not obtained confirmation from the event leader)
As well as signing up here you will need to complete the centres online health form/disclaimer (only takes a couple of mins):-
https://waiver.smartwaiver.com/w/mjvg4tugrr7udphg4v8zxr/web/?auto_tag=fh_id_220245491
This event will take place whatever the weather (as long as the river is open for watercraft) !
This will involve a drive to the canoe centre, approximately five hours of paddling down the river (this is relatively easy as we are going with the flow of the water), a visit to a nice riverside pub at the end before returning to the campsite.
The canoes are open Indian style ones. A water tight barrel is provided to keep things dry in the event of capsizing. Whilst the barrel is reasonably watertight, this cannot be guaranteed so please do not bring any valuables that are incompatible with water!
Buoyancy aides are also provided.
There may be an option on the day to use a kayak if you have some previous experience and would prefer.
Please bring snacks / packed lunch / drink for the journey and for our lunch stop by the riverside.
There is a garage shop next to the canoe centre where we start from where you can purchase some supplies prior to the trip.
Don’t forget money/card to pay for the canoe trip and  for a pint/food in the pub at the end.
If you have any queries / questions either message the event leader.
Want to Get Out More?
Check out our
South Wales & South West Calendar View
Please see our website for more
information including an FAQ
about our events.
Photo Credits
: All photos taken by OutdoorLads members.
Difficulty & Distance: You will need to be capable of paddling a canoe for 5 hours with assistance of the river flow. So some level of fitness is required with good arm/shoulder mobility.

Title: BIRMINGHAM Indoor Climbing - MONDAY - Experienced
Summary:
Join OutdoorLads for an evening at Redpoint Birmingham Climbing Centre.
"EXPERIENCED" in this instance is those that have previously climbed & can answer YES to all three of these questions:
Can you fit a climbing harness correctly?
Can you tie in to a rope using the correct knots?
Can you belay another climber correctly & confidently?
Please ensure you only sign up for this event if you
satisfy all three of the prerequisites as an experienced (independent) climber!
Never climbed before? Need a refresher on the basics? Answered NO to any of the three questions above? You will need to
attend one of the beginners (supervised) events
instead, where we will happily sign you in to be supervised (two people per leader maximum), show you the ropes & teach you everything you need to know to climb safely! Beginner (supervised) events are not offered every week.
If you are climbing with us for the first time, whether Experienced or Beginner, it is essential that you send a note to the Event Leader telling them about your previous climbing experience & your goals!
Bouldering
- Most of us do roped climbing, but if you prefer to do bouldering, you can sign yourself in & go bouldering (unsupervised) instead of, or as well as, roped climbing. Please meet the leader & the group at the start though; some others might wish to do bouldering too!
Please note that we will not teach lead climbing or lead belaying on this event -
we would suggest having some
lessons in Lead Climbing
by a qualified member of staff if this is something you're interested in pursuing!
If you are subsequently unable to attend, please cancel off the event as 'no-shows' are not only rude, but also frustrating for the leader & other attendees who may have travelled over an hour away to climb with you!
Redpoint Birmingham Climbing Centre
Indoor climbing centres offer the challenges of climbing that you'll find on natural rock crags - simply indoors! It's a fun way to stay fit, make new friends & develop your climbing skills.
Climbing
- Unlike Indoor Bouldering there is a certain amount of skill to be mastered, such as tying in knots & belaying a partner, as well as the climbs being higher than in bouldering. If you are new to climbing or would like a refresher, then this is just for you!! If you’ve climbed before, can safely tie into a harness & can competently belay, then there’s plenty of choices of top-rope & lead climbing on offer here.
Bouldering
- Instead of tackling long high routes, the 'problems' (as they are referred to) are shorter & focus on technique, balance, body positioning & sometimes power. With the different emphasis, bouldering doesn't have the hassle of climbing with ropes, so it’s excellent (but not necessarily easier) for newcomers.
Redpoint Birmingham Climbing Centre
has more than 75 lines with over 300 routes, set through some spectacular ground including its notorious 'stalactite'. It even features one of the few indoor 'Yorkshire VS cracks' in the country, to test your jamming skills. The bouldering rooms are just as interesting as its climbing routes, over 200 bouldering problems have been organised into graded circuits to provide something for everyone.
You MUST register via this event page before coming to the centre!
To climb, you will need to:
Complete a
Registration Form
(please arrive early to do this in-person the first time) & pay a Registration Fee:
Annual Registration
is
£5
or
Day Registration
is
£3
Pay an
Entry Fee
directly to the climbing centre:
Standard Entry
is
£12.50
&
Bouldering Only
is
£8.50.
Hire
climbing shoes (
£3.50
) & a climbing harness (
£3.50
) if you don't own your own.
Please see Redpoint's
website
for current prices & note that prices are subject to change.
If you have any questions or need any more information just get in touch with the event leader by clicking on their profile below, or take a look at the
My first climbing event page
for more information.
PLEASE NOTE:
All those attending the event must also be aware of the climbing centre's own terms & conditions of participation & abide by them, along with any instructions given by the centre's staff.
Photos:
taken & used with permission of
R. Casabuena
&
K. Jones
.
Difficulty & Distance: This event is for EXPERIENCED (independent) climbers only!!!

Redpoint Birmingham Climbing Centre
Indoor climbing centres offer the challenges of climbing that you'll find on natural rock crags - simply indoors! It's a fun way to stay fit, make new friends & develop your climbing skills.
Climbing
- Unlike Indoor Bouldering there is a certain amount of skill to be mastered, such as tying in knots & belaying a partner, as well as the climbs being higher than in bouldering. If you are new to climbing (or would like a refresher), there are plenty of top-rope routes to choose from after learning the essential climbing & safety techniques with one of our friendly, experienced climbing leaders.
Bouldering
- Instead of tackling long high routes, the 'problems' (as they are referred to) are shorter & focus on technique, balance, body positioning & sometimes power. With the different emphasis, bouldering doesn't have the hassle of climbing with ropes, so it’s excellent (but not necessarily easier) for newcomers.
Redpoint Birmingham Climbing Centre
has more than 75 lines with over 300 routes, set through some spectacular ground including its notorious 'stalactite'. It even features one of the few indoor 'Yorkshire VS cracks' in the country, to test your jamming skills. The bouldering rooms are just as interesting as its climbing routes, over 200 bouldering problems have been organised into graded circuits to provide something for everyone.

Title: London Bouldering Morning - All Abilities welcome!
Summary:
Come and join OutdoorLads for some bouldering in central London!
If you have not been to one of the London Climbing Centres you will need to pre-register
HERE
. You only need to register once to use any of their 8 centres in London.
This event is for members of all levels of experience and all are welcome to come along to any of the sessions.
Bouldering is a form of climbing where instead of long high routes, the 'problems', as they are referred to, tend to be shorter and focus on technique, balance, body positioning and sometimes power. Because of this different emphasis, bouldering doesn't have the hassle of climbing with ropes, harnesses and belaying, which makes the activities excellent for newcomers to the sport.
If you're a seasoned climber you won't need convincing about the benefits of bouldering, but for those who've never climbed more than a staircase, the benefits are wide ranging: from the physical - improved strength, tone, balance, power and coordination; mental - problem solving, focus, concentration and body awareness; to the emotional - relaxation, a sense of achievement and a feeling that mind and body are working together perfectly ('feeling psyched'). On top of all this, bouldering tends to be a very social sport where you can hang out with mates and each have a go as you feel regardless of what level you climb at.
First time boulderer?
If you have never tried bouldering before, the event leader can give you a short introduction to get you started and give a few a few pointers on technique. After which we will join in with the rest of the group.
All those attending the event must also be aware of the centre's own terms and conditions of participation and abide by them, along with any instructions given by the centre’s staff. Everyone must also take responsibility for their own safety while participating in Indoor climbing events.
Experienced boulderer?
Simply register with the wall and turn up on the day!
As above, all attending the event must abide by the centre's T&Cs etc. As an experienced boulderer you are at all times responsible for your own safety and so should use your own judgement in taking up advice given either by OutdoorLads volunteers or other experienced climbers.
Difficulty & Distance: With climbs at every grade from complete beginner to seasoned pro, you decide how hard it gets!

Title: Southampton August social
Summary:
We're returning to Piecaramba! for the August social - An award winning pie and mash restaurant - with over 21 pies to choose from including meat, vegetarian, vegan plus gluten free options!
We will head to a local bar in Beford Place afterwards for a drink or two!
This is a perfect event for members old and new to catch up with each other.
To see all the walking events in the southeast, please click this
link
.
Photographs courtesy of Piecaramba website / Facebook / User Geeky James
Difficulty & Distance: Social event

Title: Dolgellau Double 2: The Precipice Walk
Summary:
Route Description
The 4 mile Dolgellau Precipice Walk, as it is known locally, runs around the 12th Century Nannau estate and along the shore of Llyn Cynwch a working reservoir that provides Dolgellau with water. The path will take you through woodlands and meadows and then along a trail that clings to the edge of the hill (hence the name). Don't worry it isn't that scary! There are  captivating views of Yr Wddfa and the Moelwynion to the north, The Rhinogydd to the west, whilst Cader Idris, the Aran and Arenig ranges can be seen towards the south and east.
Although the path stands at 800ft above sea level, the starting point is already high up, so the route is relatively flat, and the reward factor vs effort expended is great. The route makes a good introduction to the area and is perfect to follow the more energetic route the day before. (see below)
After the circular route there is a hillfort on an adjacent hill to explore taking an hour or so. This part is 2 miles with 200m of height gain.  Those who prefer otherwise could be on their way home, or return to the town for tea and cake etc.
The Dolgellau Double
This event is one of 2 planned for this weekend in the area. Saturday's walk can be found at this link:

Title: Dolgellau Double 1: The beautiful Afon Mawddach
Summary:
Route Description
Our walk starts and ends in the charming and historic market town of Dolgellau (pronounced like Dol-geth-lie) which started life in the Welsh gold rush times, was famous for ship building and is now popular with hikers and climbers exploring the Cadair Idris mountain range that looms over the town. Our walk explores the land above the upper reaches of the stunning Mawddach Estuary.
Initially we will take a back lane out of the town to the small settlement of Llaneltyd where it joins with the Arfon Mawddach. We then ascend sharply up the side of Foel Ipris (328 m) to a level section on the hillside from where in good weather we will get some beautiful views down the estuary towards the coast, and of the Cadir Idris mountain range.
We descend back down through the forest and cross the river on a rustic and historic wooden toll bridge (charge
30 pence
–
please bring change
), to stop for a drink on the incredibly scenic terraces of the George III Hotel in Penmaenpool: Wales 'pub of the year'.
The last sector of the walk follows a disused railway which is now a well established cycle and walking route, and follows the Arfon Wnion back into the town. For those that wish to round off the day, Dolgellau has some great cafes and eateries.

Title: Ayr to Dunure Coastal Walk
Summary:
This is an incredible walk along one of the most rugged sections of the Ayrshire Coast. You will experience varied terrain including beaches, rocky outcrops, grassy farmland and a disused railway. Prepare yourself for stunning views and a unique sense of remoteness.
Our seven mile walk, although mostly on the flat, will take us along some rocky stretches of coastline, past harbours once used by smugglers and fishermen, sandy beaches and ruins of 16th century castles.
It is a relatively straightforward walk, but may require some care on the rockier stretches, particularly if the tide is high.
Dogs:
The walk is suitable for dogs, which must be kept under control at all times, particularly near livestock.
Photo Credits:
All with permission gillianswalks.com

Title: Fields, Woods & Wildlife: the Great Everdon Escapade
Summary:
This is a walk that may surprise anyone who is unfamiliar with this part of middle England; a beautiful 9-mile circular through the rolling hills of
South Northamptonshire
. Our walk takes in a selection of charming villages packed full of ironstone cottages, plenty of rural views, an enchanting wood and a stately home which now serves as a luxury country house hotel.
Starting on the village green in
Everdon
, we'll take the No Through Road up to the hamlet of
Little Everdon
, before cutting across the fields to
Newnham
. From there, we pick up the first mile or so of the
Nene Way
– a 110 mile path which starts in Northamptonshire and runs through Cambridgeshire and Lincolnshire all the way up to The Wash.
After skirting around
Badby
, we join the start of the
Knightley Way
– a scenic path that runs down to Towcester. One of the highlights of the walk takes us around the edge of Badby Wood – a
Site of Special Scientific Interest
and a haven for wildlife which has a history of continuous woodland cover for over seven hundred years.
Emerging from the wood, we cross over Badby Down to enjoy the first of a series of spectacular viewing spots where you can gaze at miles of unspoilt English countryside. We'll pause here for our picnic lunch, overlooking
Fawsley Hall
– a Grade I listed country hotel and spa.
Descending down past Fawsley and its tiny church which sits perched on a grassy knoll surrounded by a ha-ha to keep the cattle out, we cross the fields again and head up to the village of
Preston Capes
.
Here the gently undulating terrain becomes a little bit more challenging; after climbing up to Preston Capes and descending back down to the east of the village, we're in for a surprisingly steep climb back up to the corner of Mantles Heath. But the climb is well worth it – we're rewarded with more spectacular views looking northwards towards Daventry and Warwickshire beyond.
Having caught our breath, it's back down into the valley and across the fields to return to Everdon and an opportunity to quench our thirst at
The Plough Inn
.
Dogs are welcome:
Our walk includes some short stretches on country roads and village streets. We may encounter sheep, lambs, cows or horses and there are a few fields where landowner signs request dogs on a lead.
Waiting List:
If there are no places left on this event, please join the waiting list as people frequently drop out during the week before an event, and you are very likely to get a place.
Don't waste that space!
If your plans change and you are unable to make the event, please cancel your attendance so that others get the opportunity to sign up and make travel plans.
New Members:
Please feel free to join this as your first OutdoorLads event. If you have any questions then please
Message Me
, or go to the
My First Event
section of the website for more information.
Pictures:
All images taken by the Event Leader (April 06, 2026) with permission for
OutdoorLads
to use and reuse.
Difficulty & Distance: 9.2 miles on a mix of good field paths, quiet country roads and village streets. Total ascent: 305m / 1000ft.

Title: On the Slopes of Yr Wyddfa (Snowdon) - Eryri
Summary:
Join us for a weekend in the
Nant Gwynant
area, staying in a
recently renovated mountain bunkhouse
beneath the southern slopes of
Yr Wyddfa (Snowdon)
.
Nant Gwynant is one of the main gateways into southern Eryri (Snowdonia). The
Watkin Path starts directly outside the front door
, while Beddgelert, the Glaslyn valley and the lakes of Nant Gwynant are all within easy reach. The area combines mountains, lakes, rivers, woodland and the remains of Eryri's industrial past, making it an excellent base for a walking weekend.
One of the biggest advantages of staying here is that
all walks will start directly from the bunkhouse
, allowing us to leave the cars behind for the weekend and explore the area on foot.
We'll arrive on Friday evening, with our traditional OutdoorLads buffet to start the weekend. Breakfasts are included on both mornings, along with a shared evening meal on Saturday.
Walking
The walks offered will depend on the leaders available for the weekend.
If sufficient Mountain Walk Leaders are available, we hope to offer mountain walks on both days. Possible routes include
Yr Wyddfa (Snowdon)
via the Watkin Path and
Cnicht
.
If mountain leadership isn't available, we'll instead make use of the extensive network of paths around Nant Gwynant, Beddgelert and the Glaslyn valley. These lower-level routes still provide access to some of the best scenery in Eryri without venturing onto the higher mountains.
Final walk details will be confirmed once the event attendance is known.
The Bunkhouse
Our base for the weekend is a traditional stone mountain bunkhouse that has recently undergone a major refurbishment. It combines modern facilities with the character of a historic mountain property, creating an excellent base for exploring this part of Eryri.
Facilities include comfortable bunk rooms, a well-equipped kitchen, dining area, sitting room with a wood-burning stove, drying room and modern shower facilities. With the Watkin Path passing the front door, we can simply step outside and start walking.
The Area
The bunkhouse is ideally located for exploring the Nant Gwynant area. Nearby are the
Watkin waterfalls
,
Llyn Dinas
,
Llyn Gwynant
,
Cwm Llan
and the
Glaslyn
river, while
Beddgelert
can be reached on foot along public footpaths.
The surrounding area includes some of Eryri's best-known mountains, including
Yr Wyddfa (Snowdon)
,
Cnicht
,
Moel Hebog
and the
Nantlle Ridge
, alongside quieter valleys and woodland for lower-level walks.
Parking
Parking at the bunkhouse is very limited
, with space for around
six vehicles
, and there are no practical alternative parking options nearby. The nearest public parking is in
Beddgelert
, approximately
3.5 miles away
.
Car sharing will therefore be essential for this event.
If you can offer lifts, or would like one, please indicate this my messaged the event leader once you have booked, so we can coordinate transport before the weekend. Please consider using public transport and meeting others at a station on the way.
Staying on Sunday Night
The event officially finishes on Sunday afternoon. However, you're welcome to stay for a third night on Sunday at no additional cost if you'd like to make more of the weekend and reduce the amount of travelling.
If you choose to stay, there will usually be enough food available for breakfast on Monday morning, but an evening meal on Sunday is not included. You'll therefore need to provide your own meal, either by cooking at the bunkhouse or eating out locally.
Please let the leader know nearer the time if you intend to stay on Sunday night. A walk will still be offered on the Sunday before the event finishes.
Dogs:
This event is not suitable for dogs.
Photos:
All images are event leaders own with permission given to OutdoorLads for use and reuse
Difficulty & Distance: Walks will be of a moderate length, likely involving a full day out

Title: Knock Hill Circular Walk
Summary:
Although only a mere 217m in height, Knock Hill sits in a commanding position above the seaside town of Largs, overlooking the Islands of Cumbrae and Bute in the Firth of Clyde.
By combining both the high and low route options of the Ayrshire Coastal Path at Largs, we can enjoy this circular route to the top of Knock Hill (268m / 879 ft) and back again. An easy first stage along Largs esplanade soon gives way to farm tracks and boggy hillside paths before a steep ascent to the summit trig point. Enjoy panoramic views across the Firth of Clyde to the Cowal Peninsula, southern highlands, Isle of Cumbrae and Arran, as well as down into the town of Largs itself. The descent takes us west across farmland and through pleasant woodland then along a minor road behind Knock Castle.

Title: Castle Hill Circular Walk, Huddersfield
Summary:
A 5.4 miles (8.7 km) circular walk with approx. 1000ft (300m) of assent/descent.
A relaxed countryside walk just south of Huddersfield, West Yorkshire. We’ll wander through woodlands, stroll across open heath and farmland, and gradually make our way up to one of Huddersfield’s most iconic viewpoints - Jubilee Tower on Castle Hill.
Castle Hill provides 360 degree panoramic views sweeping across Huddersfield, with places like Leeds, Bradford, Emley Moor and Marsden in the distance.
The walk is expected to take 2-3 hours. There will also be the option of going for a pint after.
Please bring appropriate clothing and footwear.
Dogs:
Well-behaved, socialised and under-control dogs are welcome on this events. Dogs must be kept on a lead whenever we are around livestock, or when requested by the event leader.
Waiting List:
If this event is fully booked and you would like to attend, please add yourself to the waiting list. Places often become available closer to the date, and it is likely you will still get a place.
New Members:
If you are thinking of making this your first event with ODL and have any concerns or questions, please don't hesitate to drop me a message, or go to our
'My First Event
' page.
Start times:
We will aim to start on time, but appreciate different people have different needs. To accommodate this, I will aim to be at the meeting point 20 minutes before the start time (I will be wearing a bright red Outdoor Lads T-shirt). We will wait up-to 10 minutes after the start time for people to arrive before setting off.
Route mapped on OS Maps App, available from: BlobFish85
Difficulty & Distance: Easy to Moderate - established path that are steep and sometimes uneven in places.

Title: Indoor Climbing MANCHESTER - Beginners welcome!
Summary:
This event is intended as a safe space for the LGBTQ+ community; everyone will be warmly welcomed
Join us for an evening of climbing in one of the UK's top climbing centres. Whether you are new to climbing or a seasoned climber, this centre offers something for everyone with a huge selection of routes including top rope, lead and auto belay.
Prefer to boulder? That's well catered for as well, with an extensive modern bouldering area ensuring there is plenty to keep you busy, or you can swap between climbing and bouldering on the same night.
Climbing is the main focus of the event, but this is also a great chance to meet other members and build lasting friendships. Many Outdoor Lads members join through climbing, so if you're new to the group and want to know more, come along and chat over a cuppa during the tea and cake break.
What to expect on your first climb with us?
If you are an experienced climber and have passed the centre's proficiency test, we'll pair you up with a climbing buddy and you'll be out exploring the centre in no time.
If you have never climbed before or think you could do with a refresher, we offer supervised climbing sessions with one of our leaders, subject to availability. That session will focus on introducing you to climbing, making sure you get plenty of time on the wall whilst also showing you how to belay safely. Due to the centre's rules, we are limited in the number of climbers the leader is able to supervise in each session so if you think you may need a supervised session,
please contact the leader before signing up to make sure there is enough space.
As a general guide, if you answer "no" to any of the following questions, you should opt for a supervised session:
Can you fit a climbing harness correctly?
Can you tie into a rope using the correct knots?
Can you belay and lower down another climber correctly and confidently?
After a few supervised sessions, you should be ready to complete the centre's proficiency test and once that has been passed, you'll be able to climb with the rest of the group.
Everything else you need to know
You will need to register with Big Depot before your first visit to the centre but you only need to do this once. If you are an experienced climber, you can save time and do this online
here
, selecting the 18+ unsupervised option. This also applies if you just want to boulder, just select the relevant options during the registration. If you are a beginner and require a supervised session, the leader will help register you when you are at the centre.
Photos provided by Sieh and Dexmonster.
Difficulty & Distance: There is a range of routes of varying difficulty, but there should be something to suit everybody

Title: Bouldering MANCHESTER - Beginners welcome
Summary:
Join OutdoorLads For An Evening of INDOOR BOULDERING in Manchester!
This event is intended as a safe space for the LGBTQ+ community; everyone will be warmly welcomed
Bouldering is a form of climbing that focuses on shorter "problems" emphasising technique, balance, body positioning, and sometimes power, rather than long, high routes. Without the need for ropes, harnesses, and belaying, bouldering is an accessible activity for newcomers and is often considered the "purest" form of climbing.
Bouldering offers a wide range of benefits, including:
Physical:
Improved strength, tone, balance, power, and coordination.
Mental:
Enhanced problem-solving, focus, concentration, and body awareness.
Emotional:
Relaxation, a sense of achievement, and a feeling of mind-body connection.
Bouldering is also a fantastic way to meet new people and build lasting friendships, as it's an inherently social sport where climbers share tips and strategies to conquer the routes.
New to Bouldering?
If you're new to bouldering, it is recommended that you have a guided session from the event leader to learn the basics of safe bouldering. The leader can then introduce you to the rest of the group where you can refine your newly learnt skills in a social environment.
Experienced Boulderer?
If you’re new to climbing with ODL, the leader will help to introduce you to the rest of the group so you can explore the centre together.
You are responsible for your own safety and actions while bouldering. Please use your own judgement when accepting advice from OutdoorLads Volunteers or other experienced climbers, regardless of your bouldering experience.
Ready to boulder?
You will need to sign up on the ODL event page. We would ask you to do this each time so that the leader has access to important information
If you are not already registered with Rock Over Climbing, please do so
here
to save time when you arrive. You only need to do this once.
When you arrive at the centre you will need to pay for entry and hire climbing shoes if you don't own a pair. ODL members benefit from the concessionary rate so please mention this when paying.
Get bouldering! You’ll find most of the group already bouldering so feel free to join them. If you’re new to bouldering with ODL, the leader will be looking out for you so if you spot them, please go speak with them first.
Any questions?
If you have any questions, please contact the leader directly.
Photos : Copyright granted to OutdoorLads by
Sieh
and joenellist.
Difficulty & Distance: Suitable for all abilities. If you're new to bouldering, just let the event leader know and we'll get you started!

Title: Hathersage to Baslow - DVH Way Section 2
Summary:
The Derwent Valley Trust
was set up in 1996 to promote the history, arts, wildlife and leisure opportunities that surround the river Derwent along its 55 mile course through Derbyshire. The DVH Way takes us from Ladybower Reservoir in the Peak District National Park to its end at Derwent Mouth where it flows into the River Trent near Shardlow. The Duke of Devonshire opened the walk at Chatsworth House in 2003.
The route takes us through the Chatsworth estate, Derbyshire dales, towns, villages, canals and many points of interests through the
Derwent Valley Mills World Heritage Site
. There are plenty of pubs and tea shops for refreshments along the way. The route follows the riverside path through Derby City and continues southeastwards to the historic inland port of Shardlow. Journey’s end is at Derwent Mouth where the Derwent flows into the River Trent.
The route is divided into eight sections of between  ~13 km (8 miles) and ~17 km (10.5 miles), each with a clear public transport solution. All, except the first section, are linear routes. We park at the end, travel to the start, and walk back to our cars. Sections 5, 6, 7 are brilliantly served by the East Midlands Railway Derwent Valley Line
(Derby–Matlock, hourly), which runs almost parallel to the trail the whole way. For sections (2, 3, 4 and 8), we use local buses.
Each section is a walk in itself. Join one or more, as you wish, or walk all eight sections to complete the whole route through Derbyshire. Please ask other attendees for a lift if you need one.
Section 2 – Hathersage to Baslow ~14 km (8.7 miles) Total ascent 92 m (302 ft)
Near to the beginning of this section we pass by an award-winning building, The Round House cutlery factory of David Mellor, built in 1990 on the site of a former gas works. It is one of Britain's most outstanding buildings of the 20th century. Shortly afterwards we arrive at Leadmill Bridge and a former corn mill nearby.
The DVH Way follows the Derwent closely along this section. Coppice Wood is an ancient woodland, and we see views of the gritstone edges of Froggatt and Curbar in the distance. At 17th century Grindleford Bridge, a notorious outlaw called William Nevison had a narrow escape before he was later captured and hung at York in 1684. We cross over Froggatt Bridge.
The old Calver Bridge carried the turnpike road until it was swept away by a raging flood in 1799. Calver Mill, built as a cotton mill in 1778, was also party swept away in the flood. Now residential apartments, the impressive 7-storey building, with fire-proof cast iron pillers, was used as a screen set for the television series Colditz in the 1970s.
Bubnell is a pretty hamlet with a hall as approach Baslow Bridge with its tiny toll house that survived the flood. St Anne's church and its churchyard reach right up to the riverbank. Note the special VICTORIA1897 clock face.
Bridge End, the oldest part of Baslow, has galleries, shops and refreshments to offer a welcome break at the end of the walk.
There is a link to the
OS Maps
route for members, below.
Well behaved
dogs are welcome
but must kept under control at all times.
Note:
If there are no places left on this event, join the waiting list, as people frequently drop out during the week before an event, and you are likely to get a place.

Title: London Bouldering Thursday Evening
Summary:
Come and join OutdoorLads for some bouldering in central London.
This event is for members of all levels of experience and all are welcome to come along to any of the sessions.
Bouldering is a form of climbing where instead of long high routes, the 'problems', as they are referred to, tend to be shorter and focus on technique, balance, body positioning and sometimes power. Because of this different emphasis, bouldering doesn't have the hassle of climbing with ropes, harnesses and belaying, which makes the activities excellent for newcomers to the sport.
If you're a seasoned climber you won't need convincing about the benefits of bouldering, but for those who've never climbed more than a staircase, the benefits are wide ranging: from the physical - improved strength, tone, balance, power and coordination; mental - problem solving, focus, concentration and body awareness; to the emotional - relaxation, a sense of achievement and a feeling that mind and body are working together perfectly ('feeling psyched'). On top of all this, bouldering tends to be a very social sport where you can hang out with mates and each have a go as you feel regardless of what level you climb at.
Do I need a guided session?
If you have never tried bouldering before it is recommended that you have a guided session from the event leader, who will go through everything you will need to know to boulder safely before joining in with the rest of the group.
All those attending the event must also be aware of the centre's own terms and conditions of participation and abide by them, along with any instructions given by the centre’s staff. Everyone must also take responsibility for their own safety while participating in Indoor climbing events.
Independent boulderer?
As above all attending the event  must abide by the centre's T&Cs etc. As an experienced boulderer you are at all times responsible  for your own safety actions and involvement. As such, you should use your own judgement in taking up advice given either by OutdoorLads Volunteers or other experienced climbers.
Can't make this event?
We run weekly climbing and bouldering events in the South East, so why not check out what's coming up near you here:
http://outdoorlads.events/SouthEastIndoorClimbing
. Alternatively, stay updated on upcoming events using the social media channels below:
Telegram
(Climbing and bouldering only):
OutdoorLads (SE) Climbing
Facebook:
http://fb.com/OutdoorLadsSouthEast
Twitter:
@OdlSouthEast
Difficulty & Distance: With climbs at every grade from complete beginner to seasoned pro, you decide how hard it gets!

Title: An evening with Leeds Male Voice Choir
Summary:
Join us for a memorable evening as we continue to celebrate 110 years of Leeds Male Voice Choir as the choir travels west to the very edge of Yorkshire, arriving in the Pennine town of Todmorden for an autumnal concert in the splendour of Todmorden Town Hall.
With a thrilling mix of choral favourites, serene sacred music and foot taping show tunes, this is an evening not to be missed, also featuring familiar faces from OutdoorLads in the choir.
Tickets can be purchased online:
https://leedsmalevoicechoir.co.uk/tickets
or on the door. The cost is £14.00.
We will go for a drink at a nearby pub after the concert.
Image credits: Stage Shots Photography and Leeds Male Voice Choir. OutdoorLads has permission to use them.
Difficulty & Distance: Todmorden Town Hall is wheelchair accessible throughout, with accessible toilets and a lift

Title: Gay City Bowlers Leeds - July
Summary:
OutdoorLads joins the UK's premier gay bowling night, Gay City Bowlers, for a fun social night in Leeds
It doesn't matter about skill level or experience - it's a fun inclusive night! We will meet at Roxy Lanes and will be allocated into lanes for two games.
There is a friendly competition across the night and the top 3 scorers of the night win medals. After the bowling we will gather to finish off the evening with a refreshing beverage.
More details about Gay City Bowlers, including details of events in other cities, can be found on their
Instagram
page.
IMPORTANT:
You must purchase your ticket for this event through the
Gay City Bowlers website
and then
sign up on this event page
! If you don't purchase a ticket prior to the event you will not be able to take part.
The tickets are £20 for two games. £1 is added by Gay City Bowlers to support their events across the UK, you can remove this or donate more.
Interested in keeping up-to-date with GCB news? Join their WhatsApp Community Group for Leeds:
https://chat.whatsapp.com/HfJVoLq7GhW6XqgEEfzJhU?mode=ems_copy_t

Title: Fireside evenings above the Calder Valley
Summary:
A weekend retreat to remember!
Perched high above the Calder Valley, this 17th‑century Grade II listed camping barn has a real off‑the‑beaten‑track feel, surrounded by open countryside and wide Pennine views. It’s a perfect place to experience the autumn leaves and the breezes that sweep across the rolling hills.
From the barn, there are many options for nearby walks include the dramatic ridge to Stoodley Pike, loops around local reservoirs, or gentler valley walks towards Hebden Bridge and Hardcastle Crags. For those wanting something lower level, there are plenty of scenic paths straight from the door. If you’re not walking, you can head into Todmorden or Hebden Bridge for cafés, shops and a slower day, or settle in for a reading day in the large indoor activity space.
This event is a two‑night weekend retreat. Walks will take place on Saturday, with routes chosen closer to the time based on weather. It’s also a great opportunity to get involved in route planning if you’d like to contribute.
Accommodation
The barn is simple, but we’ll create fantastic memories through community, conversation and cosy fireside moments. This peaceful rural setting is a great way to experience the wilder side of West Yorkshire. Expect a couple of days of walking, cooking together and being part of a friendly, welcoming group.
There are three bunk‑bed rooms sleeping 8, 10 and 12 people, plus a fourth accessible room. No bedding is provided – you MUST bring your own sleeping bag, bedding and pillow.
We’ve got access to a well‑equipped kitchen with fridge/freezer, a lounge area, an accessible wet‑room shower, toilet blocks, and a large indoor barn space for activities. Wi‑Fi is available.
Heating is provided by wood‑pellet and multifuel stoves, and there’s also an outdoor fire pit and campfire area for relaxed evenings.
Food
Friday night will be a buffet – please bring something to share. Saturday night will be a shared meal cooked by the leader, with breakfast included on both Saturday and Sunday mornings.
Dogs:
This event is not suitable for dogs.

Title: Glentress Long Weekend
Summary:
Come and join our friends at OutMTB for a long weekend of off-road biking fun at Scotlands premier mountain biking centre.
Glentress itself is a huge trail centre, with a mixture of more traditional forest trails with lots of fun, techy features and plenty of newer, faster-flow trails.  Nearby Innerleithen also has some fantastic official trails and there are also some smashing natural trails.  The plan for the weekend is to combine some of all of these elements, although plans might change a little when we see what the weather brings!
Please note: OutMTB is aimed at the full LGBT community, so there is a chance that there may be members of other genders on this event.
This is a mountain biking trip, so a well serviced mountain bike will be required - front suspension minimum.
Current plans are:
Saturday 29th - Glentress trails.  Intermediate level ride, mixture of blue and red trails.  Likely around 25km, 800m elevation, 2-4 hours riding.
Sunday 30th - Gypsy Glen.  This is a nearby route of natural MTB trails.  This means fewer features like berms and jumps, but more unpredictable things like rocks and roots.  Comes with some excellent views!  This is also an intermediate type ride, 2-4 hours duration, 25km distance and 700m of elevation.
Sunday 31st - Innerleithen red trails: again 2-4 hours riding, 25km and around 800m elevation.
We will meet at the Glentress carpark for the Saturday event.  Should you wish to only attend the Sunday/Monday then get in touch and we will arrange a meeting point.
There are lots of accommodation options in the area.  I will be sharing a lodge at Glentress, there are also pods and camping spots and the lower part of the area, as well as plenty of hotel options either at Glentress or nearby Peebles. Bike hire is available at Glentress.
Photos:
by Marcus8
- - - - - - - - - - - - - - -
Want to keep in touch with what's going on in Scotland?
Join our Telegram
chat
and
notification
groups, and bookmark our upcoming events list via our
Scottish Calendar
.
Difficulty & Distance: A range of biking will be on offer, not super technical but things can be unpredictable.  A reasonable amount of distance and climbing.

Title: Outwood Bound
Summary:
Join Paul on this leisurely bike ride from Manchester to Bolton and back.
Everyone (including beginners) is welcome. No one will get left behind. We'll ride at a gentle pace of 8 to 10 miles per hour for about 3 hours, but the event timing is longer to allow for the natural stops and starts of the ride, plus a short lunch stop in Bolton.
There are frequent trains between Bolton and Manchester, so there's the option of leaving/joining the ride in Bolton if you fancy a shorter cycle of less than 2 hours. If joining us from Bolton, please book onto the event as usual, then message our ride leader Paul for details of where to meet and the approximate meeting time.
Route information
Starting from Central Manchester, we will ride along National Cycle Network (NCN) Route 55 to Bolton. This follows roads with cycle lanes as far as Eccles (or there's a slightly longer off-road option along canal paths and dedicated cycle paths). We'll then use the quiet roads in the leafy suburb of Ellesmere Park to get to Monton. There we join the former Monton Green to Bolton Railway line, known as the Roe Green loop line which keeps us mainly off road until we reach the centre of Bolton.
Our return leg continues mainly off road to NCN Route 6 and the Outwood Trail, another former rail ay line running between Radclliffe and Prestwich. After passing through Drinkwater Park to the outskirts of Salford, we will keep following NCN Route 6 (which has on and off road options) back to the centre of Manchester.
Although much of the route is traffic free or on quieter roads, we may encounter the odd busy road. There will also be some fairly gentle climbs along the way. See the
location
section below for a link to the proposed route map and elevation chart.
The terrain is best suited to hybrid or mountain bikes, though it can be done on a road bike. Some parts of the trail are uneven and can be muddy if wet. Please check that your bike is roadworthy before the ride, as you will be responsible for dealing with your own mechanical issues, should they arise on the day.
Weather Conditions
Cold, wet and windy weather conditions aren't conducive to sociable cycling, and part of the route has been known to flood after very heavy rain. So, if the weather forecast for the day indicates extreme conditions, the event may be postponed with as much notice as possible provided to participants.
All photos taken by OutdoorLads leaders and used with permission.
Difficulty & Distance: This is a 28 mile gentle leisure cycle. Some attendees may prefer to cycle half of the  route (14 miles) and catch a train for the other half.

Title: Cardiff Social - Part 2
Summary:
Come & join for us a night on the town in Cardiff.
This is the perfect event for members old and new to catch up with each other, find out about upcoming events and let their hair down.
We also encourage potential new members to come along and find out what we are all about. If you've not been on any of our events before, this is a great opportunity to chat to existing members and leaders about the group and to find out how friendly and welcoming we are.
If you are in any way uncertain about joining OutdoorLads, are concerned about fitness/ability or whether OutdoorLads is for you, come along, say hello, have a drink with us and judge for yourself.
We will begin our evening at the Golden Cross pub. Once the main social ends, some or all of us may well decide to head on to other bars or clubs in Cardiff.
If you have any questions before the event, please feel free to drop the Leader a message.
Join us for a night of drinks & laughter at a pub or two around Cardiff.

Title: Cardiff social P1 - Treetop Mini Golf
Summary:
Come and join us for a fun, relaxed and social evening.
We'll start the evening off with a game of mini golf. If you have never played before, no need to worry all abilities are welcome. We'll play a game of 18 holes, there are two challenges the Tropical Trail or Ancient Explorer. We'll be meeting at Cardiff's Tree Top Golf (roof of St David's car park -
www.adventuregolf.com/cardiff
). The price for playing is £13 per person. You don't need to book online for a spot or pay in advance, we just pay when we arrive. It's that simple.
We will then head to The Golden Cross for 21:00. I'll make a separate event for this for further details, so you can have the choice to do both or only one of the events.
Join us for a promising night of fun & games with a night on the town to a pub for drink or two around Cardiff.

Title: Midweek Meander - 7 Bridges and a Burger
Summary:
Join Alex for a slow mid-week walk along the Trent as it passes through the centre of Nottingham, ending up in a city centre pub for a bite to eat.
This route passes
seven bridges
, and crosses the
newest bridge
twice.  It's an out and back walk, so some of the bridges are counted twice.
We will walk from the Meadows Embankment, past Trent Bridge and loop around a lake in Colwick.
Further information about the route is available to logged in users.
If this is your first ODL event, there is information designed specifically for you on the
My First Event
page on the ODL website.
Note:
If there are no places left on this event, join the waiting list, as people frequently drop out during the week before an event, and you are likely to get a place.
Image Credits:
All images provided by the walk leader and permission is granted to ODL for their use.
Difficulty & Distance: The walk is flat the whole way but there are some ramps up over bridges.

Title: Rowing Taster Day at Derwent Rowing Club!
Summary:
A great
opportunity to try out the sport of rowing
on the river Derwent in Derby with Derwent Rowing Club , working in partnership with OutdoorLads!
This taster day is designed to give a fun, all round introduction to rowing in a friendly, small club environment. The day is suitable for complete beginners, so come along even if you've never been in a rowing boat!
Throughout the day you will experience learning on the rowing machines, an introduction to rowing equipment and getting out on the water in a variety of boats, from learner single sculls to larger crew boats. It will be fun, relaxed, informative, and a great experience for all.
The cost for the full day is £45
, paid to the club on the day. This includes all equipment and a buffet lunch. The full day will be at least 10am-4pm. As we will be booking out a full days use of the clubs equipment and catering, please only sign up for this event if you are absolutely sure you will attend!
The leaders partner, Rob Thornton, is an OutdorLads member and has been rowing for 20 years. He is a British rowing qualified coach and vice captain and club safety officer at Derwent Rowing Club, and will be running the day for us.
Tea and coffee will be available throughout the day and a buffet lunch of sandwiches/wraps provided (please let us know of any dietary requirements in advance). The event will go ahead whatever the weather, only thunder or high winds would lead to cancellation. We do row in the rain, but we have a warm clubhouse to dry out in!
Have a look at ‘instagram@derwent_rowing_club’ to get an idea of what the club is like and what the sport of rowing looks like.
On the day you will be required to sign a temporary membership form that includes a medical declaration and swim ability question. This brings you under the clubs insurance and allows us to best keep everyone safe.
If you have any questions please contact Rob at
safetyofficer@derwentrowingclub.co.uk
You will be sent the taster day pack PDF which details the location of the club, what to bring and what to expect.
All Derwent members helping on the day are volunteers. If the event fills up very quickly and has a large waitlist we will ask more volunteers to help for the day to try and increase the event size. If we are unable to do this please look out for future events, we’d love to see you on the water!
Photos:
Photos by Rob Thornton / Derwent Rowing Club and used by OutdoorLads with permission.
Dogs
: This event is not suitable for dogs.
Difficulty & Distance: New experience and energetic, but well in the grasp of most people

Title: July North-West Social: Manchester
Summary:
Join us for the July edition of the North-West Social! We are bringing the OutdoorLads community together in the heart of Manchester for a relaxed, informal evening of conversation and connection. We’ll be meeting at one of our favourite local venues,
Via on Canal Street
, starting from
7:30pm
.
What to Expect
Whether you are a long-standing member or brand new to the group, our monthly socials provide the perfect environment to meet like-minded people, share stories from recent adventures, and brainstorm ideas for future trips. We pride ourselves on being a warm, inclusive space for everyone.
If you are new to OutdoorLads, this is the perfect time to drop by, grab a drink, and introduce yourself to the group. We understand that taking the first step to join a new social circle can sometimes feel daunting—if you have any concerns regarding fitness, ability, or simply aren't sure if OutdoorLads is the right fit for you, please come along for a casual drink. It is a fantastic opportunity to see firsthand how our members form lasting friendships and to experience the welcoming culture of our community.
While the social is officially due to end at 11pm, many of our members will stay out afterwards and may even head to some of the other iconic Gay Village venues until the early hours.
If you have any questions ahead of the evening, please feel free to message the event leader directly.
Travel and Accommodation
Getting Here:
Via is centrally located in Manchester’s Gay Village and is easily accessible by public transport. We are just a short walk from Manchester Piccadilly and Manchester Oxford Road train stations. The area is also well-served by local Metrolink tram stops and numerous city centre bus routes. For those planning to drive, there are plenty of car parks available within walking distance of Canal Street. We recommend using the
Transport for Greater Manchester (TFGM)
website or app to check the latest travel updates for your journey.
Staying Overnight:
If you are travelling from further afield and looking to stay the night, Manchester city centre offers a wide range of accommodation to suit all budgets. There are many hotels located within a 5–10 minute walk of the venue, including options near Piccadilly Station and St. Peter's Square. We recommend booking in advance, particularly if your visit coincides with busy weekends or local events.
Other Events This Weekend
Looking to get outdoors before the social? You can join Kevin for the upcoming Bromley Cross and Blackrod walk, part of the Greater Manchester Ringway (GM14). You can find the full details and sign up below:
Bromley Cross & Blackrod Event
Future North-West Socials
Our North-West socials are held monthly, rotating between the vibrant cities of Manchester and Liverpool. We encourage you to join us at our next North-West Social in August, which will be held in Liverpool.
You can also explore our other upcoming social events taking place across the region
here
.
Manchester Village Pride 2026
We are thrilled to host a diverse array of events during Manchester Pride, running from Thursday, August 27 to Monday, August 31.
You can view our full Pride programme and sign up for individual events
here
.
We look forward to seeing you there!
All photos owned by and used with permission from OutdoorLads event leaders.
Difficulty & Distance: This is a purely social event with no physical exertion involved, making it suitable for everyone.

Title: Tintern Abbey and Wye Valley Camp
Summary:
Two nights camping with OutdoorLads overlooking the Wye Valley
The Wye Valley was designated an Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty in 1971 and in places not much has changed since then.   Our base for the weekend has stunning views over the valley, and is a short walk from Tintern Abbey.
The site boasts dedicated fire pits for each camping location (please do not move these and only wood purchased at site to be burnt), Toilet block with coin operated showers (£1 for 4 minutes), fridge and freezer available for use, as well as phone charging lockers.
On the Saturday you can expect a day walk leaving from the campsite, taking in the spectacular scenery of the Wye Valley and Tintern.
Awnings & Gazebo's:
An additional charge of £5 per night per awning and £7 per night per gazebo will be payable directly to the campsite if you wish to erect one.
Dogs:
Dogs are welcome on the campsite but you will be expected to be considerate of other campers and clear up behind them.  A charge of £2 per night will be payable directly to the campsite for each dog.
Campervans, Motorhomes, Caravans:
This event is suitable for tents and
SMALL
campervans only
Drones:
The use of drones is not permitted at the site.
Campfires & BBQ's
: This event will include a campfire/BBQ at the site.  Whilst our event leaders will take safety precautions there is still a risk to members. Please consider your behaviour around the campfire for your own and others safety.

Title: Bristol Balloon Fiesta
Summary:
Come and join us for a social day out to catch up with ODL members and friends whilst watching remarkable hot air balloons take flight. Watch entertainment that is being performed throughout the day and even get up close with the crew and pilots to talk with them and have a look at their ballooning equipment.
Should you choose to stay and make a full day of it, at 6pm we will witness these great balloons being prepared for flight. we'll see them inflate and watch their ascent to the skies before our very eyes.
At 9pm is the Nightglow. As the day gets darker, we will see 20 hot air balloons light up alongside the specially created soundtrack from BBC Radio Bristol.
This event is free of charge. If you wish to drive there and use their car park, you will need to pay in advance which is £5 per car. Go to the Bristol International Balloon Fiesta website for more details.
https://www.bristolballoonfiesta.co.uk/
Photos are provided by other OutdoorLads members.

.

Title: Farnham Pub Supper Social
Summary:
Join us for a relaxed pub dinner at The Lost Boy, one of Farnham’s most popular spots for good food, great drinks, and a lively atmosphere. It’s the perfect opportunity to unwind after a busy week, enjoy a meal, and catch up with fellow Outdoor Lads in a friendly and informal setting.
Expect a varied menu, a well-stocked bar, and plenty of conversation as we settle in for an easygoing evening together. As always, the focus is on good company, a welcoming vibe, and a simple recipe for a great night out: tasty food, great people, and plenty of laughs. 🍽️🍻😊
To see all the walking events in the southeast, please click this
link
.
Photographs used with permission from ODL repository.
Difficulty & Distance: Social event

Title: Farnham to Tilford: Commons, Heaths & History
Summary:
A scenic Surrey stroll from Farnham to Tilford, combining riverside paths, woodland trails, open meadows, and one of the county’s prettiest villages.
Leaving historic Farnham behind, we soon swap town streets for green commons, woodland paths, riverside meadows, and sandy Surrey tracks. Following the peaceful River Wey, this varied walk takes us through beautiful countryside to the postcard-perfect village of Tilford, with its historic bridges, village greens, and charming rural character.
Highlights include:
Farnham
– A historic market town known for its medieval castle, Georgian architecture, and attractive Castle Street.
The River Wey
– A peaceful river winding through meadows rich in wildlife, including herons, kingfishers, and dragonflies.
Tilford
– One of Surrey’s prettiest villages, famous for its medieval bridges, cricket green, and the much-loved Barley Mow pub.
Wey Valley Meadows
– Protected grasslands bursting with wildflowers in spring and alive with birds and insects throughout the summer.
Dogs:
This walk is very suitable for dogs. There’s plenty of open space, river access for a drink or paddle, and several fields with livestock (dogs on leads in these areas, please).
(All pictures used from OutdoorLads with permission)
Difficulty & Distance: Distance is 17.5 km (10.5 miles); total ascent is 273 m; terrain is undulating with one significant ascent; surfaces are dirt, sand, grass and tarmac.

Title: London Bouldering Thursday Evening
Summary:
Come and join OutdoorLads for some bouldering in central London.
This event is for members of all levels of experience and all are welcome to come along to any of the sessions.
Bouldering is a form of climbing where instead of long high routes, the 'problems', as they are referred to, tend to be shorter and focus on technique, balance, body positioning and sometimes power. Because of this different emphasis, bouldering doesn't have the hassle of climbing with ropes, harnesses and belaying, which makes the activities excellent for newcomers to the sport.
If you're a seasoned climber you won't need convincing about the benefits of bouldering, but for those who've never climbed more than a staircase, the benefits are wide ranging: from the physical - improved strength, tone, balance, power and coordination; mental - problem solving, focus, concentration and body awareness; to the emotional - relaxation, a sense of achievement and a feeling that mind and body are working together perfectly ('feeling psyched'). On top of all this, bouldering tends to be a very social sport where you can hang out with mates and each have a go as you feel regardless of what level you climb at.
Do I need a guided session?
If you have never tried bouldering before it is recommended that you have a guided session from the event leader, who will go through everything you will need to know to boulder safely before joining in with the rest of the group.
All those attending the event must also be aware of the centre's own terms and conditions of participation and abide by them, along with any instructions given by the centre’s staff. Everyone must also take responsibility for their own safety while participating in Indoor climbing events.
Independent boulderer?
As above all attending the event  must abide by the centre's T&Cs etc. As an experienced boulderer you are at all times responsible  for your own safety actions and involvement. As such, you should use your own judgement in taking up advice given either by OutdoorLads Volunteers or other experienced climbers.
Can't make this event?
We run weekly climbing and bouldering events in the South East, so why not check out what's coming up near you here:
http://outdoorlads.events/SouthEastIndoorClimbing
. Alternatively, stay updated on upcoming events using the social media channels below:
Telegram
(Climbing and bouldering only):
OutdoorLads (SE) Climbing
Facebook:
http://fb.com/OutdoorLadsSouthEast
Twitter:
@OdlSouthEast
Difficulty & Distance: With climbs at every grade from complete beginner to seasoned pro, you decide how hard it gets!

Title: Ice Skating, Optional Pub Social. All Abilities Welcome 😃

PLEASE NOTE:- THE EARLIEST YOU CAN BOOK YOUR SESSION IS 6 DAYS PRIOR TO THE DATE OF YOUR SESSION.
